description: Dependency DAG of the Proximity Prize program. Nodes = statements/artifacts/experiments; edges point FROM a dependency TO the statement it supports. gate=all: every 'req' child needed; gate=any: one 'alt' child suffices (plus all 'req'). Statuses: PROVED (on main w/ proof or exact verification), PROVABLE (route pinned, write-up pending), CONDITIONAL (proved under stated hypothesis), CONJECTURE (precise statement, unproved), TARGET (fuzzy, expected to sharpen), WALL (frozen hard core w/ consumer theorem), REFUTED (dead route), TEST (experiment/prediction). Source of truth for statuses: experimental/notes/roadmaps/ (r2 + proof_sketch + wp_detail). REFINEMENT PRINCIPLES (2026-07-02): (1) critical nodes carry exact 'statement' fields (+ attack_surface/falsifier notes) — machine-enforced; (2) new conjectured nodes are admitted ONLY as branches of exhaustive splits, proved equivalences/reductions, or with a named non-ev consumer + truth-confidence note — never as speculative lemma chains presuming a proof shape; (3) every critical conjecture gets a cheap wired falsifier TEST. INTEGRATED UPSTREAM 2026-07-02 (commit 01add412, PR #174 closed-integrated): this DAG is in-tree subordinate roadmap material; towards-prize.md is the source of truth.

nodes.title: F WLOG reduction: factor out the plane's common divisor
nodes.statement: Let w = deg gcd(P cap D_j). Division by the gcd maps P cap D_j injectively into P' cap D'_{j-w} for an explicit linear P' of dim <= dim P over the co-domain (X^n-1)/gcd, with trivial image gcd. Hence F reduces to gcd-trivial planes; the deep-gcd branch IS the tangent shape.
nodes.notes: elementary: multiples-of-ell_W form a subspace; division is linear | IN FLIGHT: PR #182 (Codex) proves this (verifier replayed green locally, 13792 reduction checks; tip adds a bonus common-root consumer bound). nodes.title: F scale recursion: pullback points are plane points of the quotient instance
nodes.statement: P cap D_j^per(M) is linearly isomorphic to P' cap D'_{j/M} at scale n/M (an M-pullback ell = g(X^M) places g's coefficients at multiples of M — a linear embedding). Hence F(n) <= F_primitive(n) + sum over M | gcd(n,j) of F(n/M): the periodic branch recurses, bottoming out at checkable scales.
nodes.notes: the same multi-scale self-similarity as Q_M = Q_1 and the coset-move dynamics — third appearance of the recursion | IN FLIGHT: PR #182 (Codex) proves this (verifier replayed green locally, 13792 reduction checks; tip adds a bonus common-root consumer bound). Flips to PROVED on integration. | NEEDS THE DIHEDRAL ANALOGUE: plane sections of the reciprocal stratum should be plane sections of the Chebyshev-quotient instance (y = x + x^{-1}), mirroring the multiplicative case — plausibly provable identically (the X^m h(X + X^{-1}) factorization is the pullback map). | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

The historical pattern holds: taxonomy grows, completeness persists. | E35: taxonomy clause correction (projective multiplicative form X^e g(X^M)); no new mechanism — the example is a twisted pullback, inside the symmetry sector. Fifth-shape watch remains clean across E25/E27/E30/E32/E35/E36. | THIRD QUOTIENT-CLAUSE REFINEMENT (X-4): the paid quotient shape must include MIXED/TAIL forms L_B(X) G(X^M), |B| < M (and the dihedral analogue) — pure pullbacks miss the staircase. Clause history: dihedral branch (X-2), projective twist X^e (E35), tail forms (X-4). Each found by counterexample, each inside the symmetry/quotient sector, fifth-shape watch STILL clean. | S9 FIRES — THE FIFTH MECHANISM IS REAL (first genuine one; six prior clean probes were structurally blind at weight > 4): primitive moment-trade blocks cancel without symmetry. The 'cancellation = symmetry' thesis is REFUTED (my X-4b framing, falsification #5); the Dih_n keystone still governs the SYMMETRY sector, but cancellation is strictly bigger. MCA-side compiler survives AGAIN via the slope/support distinction — the moment staircases inflate per-slope lists, not slope counts. | TAXONOMY FINAL FORM CANDIDATE (the unification): tangent + PULLBACK (the Luroth lattice: rational-map fibers with coefficient constraints — subsumes multiplicative, dihedral, moment-trade/PTE, and the affine-involution witness as one class, complete by Luroth's theorem) + extension. nodes.title: F at dimension 1: pencils meet D_j in at most n/j points (VOTING)
nodes.statement: For a gcd-trivial pencil l_0 + t l_1: each x in H roots the member at a UNIQUE parameter t_x = -l_0(x)/l_1(x) (shared roots excluded by gcd-triviality; l_1(x)=0 gives no vote). A D_j member needs j votes; n votes exist. Hence <= n/j members (+1 for the leading-coefficient degeneracy). Far below the n^{B_F} target.
nodes.notes: elementary; the base case of F is TRUE with room to spare | IN FLIGHT: PR #182 (Codex) proves this (verifier replayed green locally, 13792 reduction checks; tip adds a bonus common-root consumer bound). Flips to PROVED on integration. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: F-primitive = j-fold concurrency of the evaluation-hyperplane arrangement (exact reformulation)
nodes.statement: On an r-flat P of parameter space, member-at-t has root x iff t lies on the hyperplane H_x = {P(x) = 0}. P cap D_j corresponds EXACTLY (up to the leading-coefficient hyperplane) to points lying on >= j of the n evaluation hyperplanes {H_x : x in H}. F-primitive <=> poly bound on j-fold concurrency points, with tangent shape = flats near {multiples of l_S} and periodic shape = pullback flats.
nodes.notes: turns F into finite-field incidence geometry: concurrency loci of a Vandermonde-type arrangement — a searchable literature (multiplicity incidence bounds, Furstenberg-type sets) | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.statement: Define E_3(u,v) = the 3-fold iterated exchange correlation (odd-order Johnson-scheme sum; E_2 = averaged_xr). Prove the EASY direction of the inverse: folded/equivariant words and tangent pairs have E_3 large (explicit lower bounds). Consistency check: E_2 reproduces the averaged-XR variance.

Structured >> random baseline confirmed on the walk-energy proxy. | DELIVERED (#184 retitled 'prove XR exchange-energy calculus'): flips on integration.

Hence in ANY dyadic prime range, all but a poly-sized exceptional set of primes have collision-light e1 value sets. Partial (the official p could be exceptional) but dossier-grade.

nodes.title: Random-prime typicality model for zone-(b) collisions (grounded by E1)
nodes.statement: The official-scale prime behaves collision-typically: #collisions at quotient order N' matches the random-prime expectation #pairs * E[Omega(Norm)] / pi(range) — negligible at the open cells (N' >= 128, log2 A >= 100, log2 p ~ 250), and with collision ONSET at the birthday scale of a 2^49.7 support for the feasible N'=64 cell (~2^25 samples). Via collision_norm_criterion + thm:upstairs this is exactly 'p divides none of the bounded-height difference norms beyond chance'.

nodes.falsifier: CORRECTED: the N'=64 cell tests NOTHING (fullness there is unconditionally forced by graded_collision_radius — E1's zero collisions were a theorem, not evidence). The model's support is the density theorem + the R4 conspiracy search; no sampling test reaches its open content at N' >= 128.

Per-leaf FM bounds over-aggregate by q^{M-1} when M > t: the bound must be joint. nodes.statement: Primitive (gcd-trivial, aperiodic) plane counts obey the weighted pair bound: #primitive(P cap D_j) <= C * PairBound(P) with small C — the E7 census sits at ~60% of the floor in both the exact census (38/60) and the kernel sample (13/30). Current per-rate corridor widths are 1.12-2.17 grid steps: even with every mapped conjecture closed, the slack must be eaten by (i) the Acl second-order term (makes the quotient end a number), (ii) per-point window cleanup at the boundary, and (iii) the extension-crossing arithmetic — or the determination lands with a gap and only bracket-grade partials. The previous unscoped wording over-claimed.

The node's 'or the determination lands with a gap and only bracket-grade partials' clause is now the live branch unless a fourth mechanism is found. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 6): TARGET -> CONDITIONAL — the assembly implication packaged; closes when the named frontier inputs land. | AMBER AUDIT 2026-07-06: REPAIRED (rate-scope split; full-vs-clean conflation found by the overnight audit). | STRESS-VERIFIED 2026-07-06 (independent second audit): implication checked B-given-A and stressed numerically/algebraically -- see notes/amber_audit_20260706/stress_verification.md.

nodes.title: B_C is an integer staircase: the adjacent crossing EXISTS; corridor = candidate list
nodes.statement: B_C(a) is nonincreasing and integer-valued in a, so a unique a* with B_C(a*-1) > B* >= B_C(a*) exists UNCONDITIONALLY for every row. The corridor arithmetic already brackets a* to at most ceil(width)+1 = 2-3 candidate grid points per rate. The endgame is therefore a FINITE list of pointwise decisions, not an analytic meeting of bounds.
nodes.notes: elementary; reframes adjacency_closing from 'no mechanism' to 'decide <= 3 points per rate' | IN FLIGHT: #193 | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: Consecutive grid counts differ by ~q-factors: sign decisions need only coarse relative precision
nodes.statement: At consecutive grid points t changes by 1, so the leading-order stratum counts jump by factors ~ q (from the exact C(n,j) q^{1-t} shape and the Acl formulas). Hence B* is crossed in ONE step, and each candidate-point comparison B_C(A) vs B* is decided by relative precision far coarser than the step factor — EXCEPT on a knife-edge set where the count sits within the estimate's error band of B*.
nodes.notes: the reason the endgame is tractable: steep staircases make comparisons cheap | IN FLIGHT: #193 | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

The exactly-zero range begins at A_zero = A*+2, after applying fm1 and the compiled clean-rate R2 bound. Thus the adjacency machinery must decide A*+1 with a live aperiodic term; it may use exact zero only from A*+2. RATE SCOPE (amber audit 2026-07-06): this node makes no rate-1/2 claim; the rate-1/2 thin point is carried by rate_half_band_closure.

The critical path now flows through r2_clean_rates <- the proved xr engines + xr_clean_residual_any_gate <- the smallcore/spread chain <- THE TERMINAL CLUSTER — the dichotomy/A3/C1 campaign is now visibly the safe side's aperiodic engine. | AMBER AUDIT 2026-07-06: REPAIRED (rate-scope split; full-vs-clean conflation found by the overnight audit). | STRESS-VERIFIED 2026-07-06 (independent second audit): implication checked B-given-A and stressed numerically/algebraically -- see notes/amber_audit_20260706/stress_verification.md.

nodes.title: The genuine residue: census + exact handling of knife-edge rows
nodes.statement: REFINED: with bounded scales + exact counts, the census is exact Diophantine arithmetic — windows [L(n,A), K(n,A)) in B*-space per rate/candidate, undecided rows = admissible primes landing inside. Ties exist systematically (B* sweeps all integers as q varies); the residual undecided set shrinks exactly as certification strength L grows toward K. Full grand-challenge closure requires L within O(1) of K at the tie point OR per-prime typicality; exhibited-row partials dodge entirely (census_dodge_selection).
nodes.attack_surface: needs acl_second_order first (the error band's width); then the census is mechanical over the descriptor family
nodes.falsifier: a dense knife-edge family (would mean the expansion's error model is wrong, not that adjacency fails)
nodes.notes: PROMOTED red -> amber (Codex flip packet, my replay 8/8; flip_packets/knife_edge_census.md): statement-level assembly over the wired kids.

nodes.title: Certified per-prime value-set LOWER bounds at the crossing scale (the knife-edge core)
nodes.statement: For a knife-edge row (specific p): certify |{e1(B) mod p}| > B* by exhibiting a provably all-pairs-distinct class family of size > B*. Collision-monotonicity makes the safe side free; THIS is the entire hard half. Per-prime (almost-all-primes tools do not apply); sampling unreachable at N' >= 128.
nodes.attack_surface: compose pairwise height-certified distinctness (norm < p => no collision) into large families: a packing/coding problem in the swap metric — minimum-swap-distance families whose difference heights stay below p, or a smarter invariant certifying blocks at once
nodes.falsifier: n/a (a construction target; its absence just leaves knife-edge rows undecided)
nodes.notes: the knife-edge census's difficulty CONCENTRATED into one object; everything else in Tier A is estimates + elementary structure | FLIP on replay (Codex critical-walk batch): assembly/conditional packet in nodes/certified_valueset_lower/conditional.md.

nodes.title: FREE RUNG: single-swap collisions are impossible at prize scale (unconditional)
nodes.statement: For classes differing in one element, e1(B) - e1(B') = zeta^i - zeta^j has all archimedean conjugates <= 2, so its norm is <= 2^{phi(N')} ~ 2^{N'/2} << p ~ 2^250. Nonzero (thm:upstairs) and of height below p => p cannot divide it => single-swap pairs NEVER collide at prize scale. Unconditional, for every prime in the admissible range.
nodes.notes: the base case of certified distinctness: the swap-metric graph's edges are all collision-free; the open problem is composing this into large all-pairs-distinct families | Subsumed as the s=1 case of graded_collision_radius; kept as the write-up's base case. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: The deciding scale is PINNED in an absolute window (n-uniformity of the census)
nodes.statement: At agreement A the quotient stratum on scale N' has forced class ratio l'/N' = j/n, so counts grow strictly as 2^{N' H(j/n)} with H(j/n) bounded below on the corridor (j/n ~ 0.4-0.5 => H >= 0.97). Hence exactly ONE scale's count lands within the staircase-step factor of B*, and it lies in an ABSOLUTE window N' in [~120, ~400] — independent of n and k up to 2^40. The census is n-uniform because every crossing only sees bounded scales.
nodes.notes: the lemma that makes a symbolic census possible at all; also gives UNIQUENESS of the deciding scale (monotone counts, q-factor gaps) | FLIP on replay (consolidation agent, verifier replayed): PROVABLE -> PROVED (unique deciding scale in N [120,400], n-uniform).

nodes.title: Exact bignum evaluation at bounded scales: expansions unnecessary at candidates
nodes.statement: With N' <= ~400 absolute, the deciding class counts K are exactly computable big integers (binomial-sum sized ~2^400 at worst). At candidate points, EXACT K replaces the leading+second-order expansion entirely: the knife-edge condition sharpens from 'estimate cannot decide' to the exact Diophantine condition B* = floor(q/2^128) in [L, K), where L is the certified lower bound strength. (acl_second_order remains needed only for corridor WIDTH/candidate localization, not for tie decisions.)
nodes.notes: PROVED (my lane, exact computation, verifier green): exact table computed at all four rates; rate-1/2 shortfall 3.8 bits below gate; rate-1/4 gate in the staircase gap.

nodes.title: The undecided set as an explicit function of certification strength L
nodes.statement: For each (rate, candidate A): undecided rows = {admissible q = 1 mod n : floor(q/2^128) in [L(n,A), K(n,A))} — explicit prime-counting windows. Deliverables: the window list, size estimates, and the dodge complement. As L -> K the windows vanish; the census OUTPUT is the map from certification strength to residual undecided rows. | PRIME-POWER EXTENSION (2026-07-07, mechanical item from the circle-scope audit; nodes/census_window_arithmetic/notes/prime_power_windows.py, VALIDATED formula == brute force on a toy window): the admissible-row quantifier extends to prime powers q = p^e = 1 mod n (< 2^256). Exact residue arithmetic for n = 2^s: #{r mod 2^s : r^e = 1} = gcd(e,2)*gcd(e,2^(s-2)); e odd forces p = 1 mod n (ALL such rows base-domain, d = 1, non-generating); e = 2 has 4 classes {+-1, 2^(s-1)+-1} of which only p = 1 mod n is non-generating (p = -1 mod n = the m2_antinorm_lever family, generating). Every window's undecided-row list gains these (e, d = ord(p mod n)) strata; non-generating rows (d < e) leave the direct corridor and are priced by the f1/ext import — the beta-lesson (catches #11-13) at the census level.
nodes.notes: couples the census to certified_valueset_lower parametrically instead of blocking on it | IN FLIGHT: #195 | Family now definitively pinned (2-power cosets, k <= 2^40, |F| < 2^256): the exposure census is EXACT finite arithmetic — compute the actual number of knife-edge-exposed cells and their prime-window sizes per rate. Coset domains: collision structure identical (multiplication by gamma is a bijection mod p) — one-line lemma to include. | PROVED (my lane, exact computation, verifier green): the L -> window map with K exact; dodge complement cited.

nodes.title: List staircase: sup-over-words list size is a monotone integer => unique crossing exists

The observed challenger is structured (not a fifth-mechanism signal); the endgame arithmetic gains one priced column rather than an unpriced supremum.

nodes.title: F at dimension 2: elementary pair bound + twins are tangent-paid
nodes.statement: On a gcd-trivial plane P: (i) domain traces H_x cap P are genuine lines; (ii) DISTINCT-trace pairs meet in one point, so sum of C(mult,2) <= C(n,2), giving #(P cap D_j) <= C(n,2)/C(j,2) for twin-free planes — elementary double counting; (iii) TWINS (coincident traces x,y) force every member through x to pass through y: the sub-pencil shares divisor (X-x)(X-y) and f_gcd_reduction pays it as tangent. F-dim-2 = pair bound + twin reduction, with the twin count T entering as (C(n,2) + (q+1)T)/C(j,2).
nodes.attack_surface: write-up + verifier; TESTABLE PREDICTION against E7's own artifact: the j=5 kernel-sample top planes (primitive max 13 > 12 = C(16,2)/C(5,2)) must contain >= 1 twin pair — count twins in the recorded top planes
nodes.falsifier: a twin-free plane exceeding C(n,2)/C(j,2) (would break the double count — arithmetic, not conjecture, so a hit means an error in the skeleton)
nodes.notes: explains the E7 numbers: 38 <= 40 at j=3 (bound TIGHTER than the packet's floor 60); the kernel excess 13 > 12 is the twin signature | CROSS-POLLINATION: #183's 'Align E7 evidence with fixed-dimension theorem' commit followed this node's publication on the #178 branch — the census packet is being aligned to the pair-bound frame. | E10 PREDICTION CONFIRMED (#183): 5/5 sampled j=5 top kernel planes contain twins; ZERO twin-free planes above the simple bound 12. Full j=4 Grassmannian census (25,734,890 planes): primitive max 28 vs simple floor 20 vs pair floor 40, all 9 top primitive planes twin-rich (2^8 profile). | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: The divisor-depth induction: excess concurrency buys shared-divisor depth, one unit per dimension
nodes.statement: F_r for gcd-trivial r-flats by induction: at each multiplicity->=j point, either the hyperplanes through it are SPREAD (pair/r-tuple counting binds — now PROVED as f_spread_descent_dichotomy L1+L2) or RANK-DEFECT coincidence (corrected from 'twin-type': equal hyperplanes OR a common pencil/subflat) forces a shared divisor and strict descent (PROVED, L3). REMAINING CONTENT (the honest residue): the GLOBAL PACKING STEP — the spread bound carries an n^r-type numerator; the induction closes only if f_scale_recursion (or a packing argument) absorbs the r-tuple count into an absolute exponent B_F.
nodes.attack_surface: REGIME-CORRECTED (Brief E): the induction/packing bound is for PROPER low-dim planes dim P << j (the applied fiber-plane regime), NOT the degenerate full-space j=dim P case. Close the packing step in that regime via f_scale_recursion + the spread bound (now non-vacuous).
nodes.falsifier: toy r = 3 census (extend E7) exceeding the compounding-depth prediction
nodes.notes: PRO BRIEF B (partial, certified): dichotomy + descent PROVED and banked as f_spread_descent_dichotomy; the twin-only reading REFUTED in dim >= 3 (statement corrected); the residue is exactly the global packing step. | FLIP on replay (Codex critical-walk batch): assembly/conditional packet in nodes/f_dim_induction/conditional.md. | BRIEF E (Pro, target B): the rational-normal/MDS flat P=span{1..X^r} at j=r (the FULL ambient P^j) has #(P cap D_r)=C(n,r) >> n^{B_F}, gcd-trivial, no spread reuse — REFUTES the absolute-B_F bound AS STATED. But this is the DEGENERATE j=dim P case where the spread bound C(n,r)/C(j,r) is VACUOUS (=C(n,r)) and conj_f's forced shared divisor has degree j-dim P = 0. The applied regime is dim P << j (low-dim fiber/kernel planes in high-degree ambient) where C(n,r)/C(j,r) BITES (verified 120/10, 120/28...). FIX: the bound holds in the regime dim P <= j-1 (proper planes); the full-space case is out of Conjecture F's scope. FLAG: verify f_primitive_case carries this regime qualifier (content audit).

nodes.title: The frame: trace degeneracies = low-weight words of the flat's dual code P-perp
nodes.statement: For a flat P, a subset S of domain points has dependent traces iff P-perp (dual of the evaluation code) contains a word supported on S. Weight 1 = common root (gcd), weight 2 = twins, weight w = the general obstruction; minimal support => all coordinates nonzero => the CLOSURE property (a member vanishing on all-but-one of S vanishes on all of S). If the dual distance w* > r, ALL r-subsets of traces are in general position.
nodes.notes: unifies gcd-reduction (w=1) and twins (w=2) as rungs of one ladder; the syndrome machinery is already dual-code language | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 4): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: Dually-spread flats: r-wise moment count gives C(n,r)/C(j,r) — poly to LOG dimension
nodes.statement: If w*(P-perp) > r: every r-subset of traces meets in exactly one point, so sum of C(mult, r) <= C(n,r), giving #(P cap D_j) <= C(n,r)/C(j,r) ~ (n/j)^r. HONEST SCOPE: poly(n) only for r = O(log n); in-band deficiencies r ~ n 2^-9 exceed this — high dimension MUST route through descent/structure, not moments.
nodes.notes: FLIP on replay (Codex wave 4): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: The descent step: a sparse dual word buys divisor depth faster than it costs dimension
nodes.statement: Given a minimal weight-w dual word on S: (a) members containing S in their roots form a subflat of codim <= w-1 (the relation makes one vanishing condition redundant) and factor as l_S x (degree j-w instance) — DEGREE drops w, DIMENSION drops <= w-1: net one unit of divisor depth gained, exactly F's j - dim P threshold shape; (b) by closure, all other members have <= w-2 roots in S: recurse on H minus S with root budget j-w+2. Single-step accounting elementary; assembly belongs to f_dim_induction.
nodes.notes: FLIP on replay (Codex wave 4): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: THE RESIDUE: flats with many sparse dual words are quotient/pullback or tangent structured
nodes.statement: RESTATED (second revision, after the verified dihedral counterexample): many-sparse flats are MULTIPLICATIVE-pullback-structured, DIHEDRAL/Chebyshev-quotient-structured (X^e g(X^M + X^{-M}) — the new class), tangent-structured, or descent-reducible. The conjecture: nothing UNPAID survives all FOUR branches. The dihedral branch is forced: reciprocal flats are gcd-trivial, multiplicatively aperiodic, and have super-poly lattices — only the enlarged quotient class pays them.
nodes.attack_surface: coding-theory structure: codes with many minimal low-weight words are classified in known regimes; the coset-support pattern (supports = unions of coset pairs) is the signature to force; toy-enumerable falsifier at n = 16

The PROVED status is legitimate as modus-ponens over the wired predicates, but consumers must NOT use this node hereditarily at w>=3 strength (the Hankel v4 lesson). nodes.title: Graded radius: collisions require swap distance > d*(N',p); small scales unconditionally FULL
nodes.statement: Pairs at swap distance s have e1-difference height <= 2s, norm <= (2s)^{phi(N')}; if (2s)^{phi(N')} < p the pair is collision-free (nonzero by thm:upstairs, norm below p). At N'=128, p ~ 2^250: d* = 7. COROLLARY: when 2l' < p^{1/phi(N')} (true at N' <= 64 for prize p), ALL pairs are certified — unconditional fullness, the prop:qfloor norm-threshold mechanism restated as certification. Subsumes single_swap_injectivity as the s = 1 case.
nodes.notes: PROVED-IN-FLIGHT: #206 (s=1 rung subsumed; exact norm formula Phi_M(1)^(phi(N)/phi(M))) | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: IMPOSSIBILITY: pure height certificates cannot reach full strength at N' >= 128
nodes.statement: SHARPENED to the proved form (#206): the pure height gate cannot certify a full quotient cell once (2 s_max)^phi(N') exceeds the field ceiling — and in the official-rate table N' = 128 already exceeds p < 2^256 for ALL FOUR rates. Any full-strength certification at in-corridor scales must use p-specific input. (Companion anchor, same PR: at most TWO primes in range can collide even half the rate-1/2 cell pairs — the exceptional set is provably tiny.)

nodes.title: THE RESIDUE: generator-factored difference designs + per-row generator certification
nodes.statement: Construct class families F with |F| > B*/poly whose pairwise e1-differences all factor as (unit of height < p^{1/phi}) x (one of g <= poly generators). Then g per-row valuation checks certify ALL pairs of F simultaneously — constant per-row computation replacing 2^244 pairwise checks. The open problem is the DESIGN: Sidon-flavored difference structure in Z[zeta_N'] compatible with the class-to-e1 map. DECOMPOSED: = cluster_certificates (free cliques + one-check pairing + integer freebies, PROVABLE) + generator_economy (multiplicative difference-designs — the sharpened open core, with the root-difference factorization as its proved germ).

nodes.title: Cluster machinery: free cliques + one-check cluster pairing
nodes.statement: (a) Diameter-d* class sets are fully certified free cliques (~2^33 at N'=128) by graded_collision_radius. (b) For clusters with center-difference Delta EVERYWHERE-BIG (min conjugate >= spread/(budget-1)): every cross-pair diff = Delta(1 + Delta^{-1} eta) with quotient norm < p, so ONE precomputable norm check on Delta certifies ALL cross-pairs of the two clusters. (c) FREEBIE: integer factors are self-certified (p does not divide integers < p), so integer multiples of certified elements are certified.

nodes.title: THE CORE, SHARPENED: multiplicative difference-designs (few bases certify everything)

PROVED GERM: root differences factor as zeta^b (zeta^k - 1): the full difference set of mu_N' is certified by g = N' bases. The open problem: lift this factorization from single roots to l'-sums. ADMISSIBILITY GUARD: the design's existence proof must not consume fiber counts (circularity with perfiber).

Next attacks: multiplicative template compression or imported abelian difference-set designs. No fiber counts used (guard respected). | SCOPED by profile_covering_obstruction: only engineered (flat-profile/integer-factored) designs can work; the kernel-lattice route (lattice_cone_certificate) is now the primary alternative — neither pure existence nor pure construction, but per-row CERTIFICATION. | CONDITIONAL (Pro Brief F, verified construction): antipodal zero-sum padding gives an O(N)-base-certified family of size 2^65.7/2^130; conditional only on the size-budget match (generator_size_budget_check).

nodes.title: Anticode/EKR toolkit for exchange-distance independence — with the honest spectral scoping
nodes.statement: Instantiate Delsarte/Frankl-Wilson anticode bounds on J(n,j): sets of locators pairwise exchange-distance > s have size <= C(n-s, j-s)-type bounds — factors (j/n)^s relative to C(n,j). SCOPING NEGATIVE (included): the Johnson gap n is tiny vs degree ~n^2/4, so single-step expander mixing is near-vacuous, and pure combinatorial distance bounds CANNOT reach q-power (FM) scales — the q-scale input must be algebraic (v8 per-locator cap, exchange residual systems). Prevents doomed naive-EML attempts.
nodes.notes: PARTIALLY DELIVERED in flight: PR #191's Johnson exchange-mixing lemma proves the single-step bound with the predicted (1 - n/(j(n-j))) factor TIGHT (point-dictators attain equality) plus the geometric endpoint decay lambda*^s — the spectral half of this toolkit.

nodes.title: Reformulation: descent-tree size = closed-set count of the sparse-support lattice
nodes.statement: The naive descent tree is binary (2^depth danger), but tree nodes correspond to CLOSED SETS of the lattice generated by sparse-word supports (unions/closures = subflats + residual domains), with chain length <= dim P + deg drop. Tree size <= lattice size — the accounting identity that makes polynomial termination plausible at all, and the object the family bounds must count.

nodes.title: Hankel-kernel flats: coset-patterned supports => divisor-poset-sized lattice
nodes.statement: SATURATED formulation (Pro round 4, verified): for rate-1/2 Hankel-kernel instances over mu_n, after ROOT-CLOSURE saturation (count reachable forced-root closures rcl_P(B u S), NOT raw support unions) and after routing tangent/common-divisor and quotient/pullback/DIHEDRAL structure to the paid/reduced ledgers, the number of reachable UNPAID PRIMITIVE saturated closures is poly(n); AND terminal unpaid primitive leaves satisfy the direction-dual (moment/residue) cleanliness. Raw #L over-counts: the loop-free pair-family (weighted syndrome on non-coset A) is gcd-trivial AND primitive with raw #L = 2^|A|, yet ALL pairs share ONE root-closure = A (verified: 5 raw -> 1 closure). The 'many-atoms => paid' heavy lifting is f_many_sparse_structure (PROVED, four-branch crystallization).
nodes.attack_surface: v5 (post-critique): four lemmas — L1 descent stability PROVED (syndrome convolution, 1800/1800); L2 paid Luroth/rational-pullback quotient reduction (incl. the missing dihedral y=X+1/X reduction); L3 the HEREDITARY closure-entropy bound (THE core — local few-atoms does not imply poly reachable closures, per QF.12's own non-claims); L4 moment-clean leaves / pinned-value variant. f_many_sparse_structure supports the weight-2 branch (constant threshold via E35) but its w>=3 branch routes to Face-4 wall machinery — insufficient for hereditary use.
nodes.falsifier: a kernel-flat sparse word with non-coset support in the restricted census
nodes.notes: E17 verdict: coset prediction FALSE; adjacent-exponent counterexample recorded with certificate. Pre-registered response executed: fall back to the general lattice argument. Falsification count for my leaps now 2 (of 5) — the cycle is honest. | LEVERAGE +4 (top of board). Reframed to the exact #closed-sets-poly question. Phase 0 probe found the object lives at rank-drop slopes — census must use the fiber machinery, not naive sampling. Pipeline banked. | PHASE 0 DONE: M(Z) pinned (v12 extractor), E17 reproduced (gate passed). REFRAMED: generic supports uniform-diameter but <=1/flat (trivial lattice); termination = MAX #closed-sets over rare structured flats. Modal census dispatched (max-search). | BRIEF FALSIFIED (Pro, #16 of my leaps; verified #L=64840): my W(P) = 'all minimal dependent supports' was too broad — it counts the GENERIC (weight = dim+1) circuits every flat has by dimension. Pro's P = span{1,X,X^2} (MDS RS_3) then gives #L = 2^n - O(n^3), exponential. FIX (Pro's own diagnosis, confirmed): W(P) = EXCEPTIONAL circuits of weight <= dim(P) only (sub-generic dependencies); MDS/general-position flats have W(P) = empty, #L = 1 — consistent with f_termination_mds PROVED. The node's termination question STANDS with the corrected W(P); the Modal max-search already uses ~the right (sub-generic) filter. Corrected brief issued. | BRIEF FALSIFIED TWICE MORE, RESOLVED: (Pro #17) weight-1 common roots give #L=2^|A| — stripped by f_gcd_reduction. (mine #18) gcd-trivial PULLBACK {g(X^2)} gives #L=2^{n/2} via disjoint {x,-x} coset-pairs — SURVIVES the gcd-strip, needs f_scale_recursion (non-pullback). VERIFIED both (#L=8, #L=256 at n=16). RESOLUTION: the true statement is f_primitive_case for Hankel — gcd-trivial + PRIMITIVE. Both proved reductions strip exactly the two counterexample classes; the downstream conj_f already consumes precisely this strength. Neither counterexample touches the primitive residual = the genuine open question. | PRO ROUND 4 (reformulation, verified): raw #L is the wrong invariant — count ROOT-CLOSURES (saturated states). Loop-free pair-family refutes even v3 (primitive) at raw level, but collapses under rcl. The node is now an ASSEMBLY: f_many_sparse_structure (PROVED) + root-closure accounting + moment-clean leaves. CAVEAT (Pro): f_support_lattice bounds state size NOT fiber count — leaf bound separate. Connects to the SPI/XR crystallization core (spi_exceptional_class). f_many_sparse_structure now LOAD-BEARING — flag for content audit. | ROUND 5: Pro's critique of v4 adjudicated LARGELY CORRECT (gap 3 the standout: my contrapositive overreached — QF.12 Remark 4.7's non-claim). Descent stability PROVED (new lemma). The four-lemma v5 decomposition adopted. f_many_sparse_structure's w>=3 Face-4-wall routing = the sharpened content-audit flag. | ROUNDS 6-7 (the same Pro thread): L3 SOLVED-IN-ROUTE — the rank-profile dichotomy (row-deficient => principal GRS via binary apolarity, verified 4000/4000; wide => one rational class, separation verified 233k pairs/0 violations; narrow => dim O(W)) gives n^{O(W^2)} unpaid states via entropy/Kraft. Node CONDITIONAL on the two packets; L2's Chebyshev syndrome transform delivered en passant; the w>=3 many-sparse inverse NOT needed. Falsification count intact: my 'bounded branching x short chains' shortcut was invalid (Pro caught it); the entropy formulation replaced it.

nodes.title: Bridge 1: E_3-large = walk-trapping = non-expanding (elementary spectral bookkeeping)
nodes.statement: E_3(A) = <1_A, (P_A M P_A)^3 1_A> (the cubed restricted one-exchange operator), so E_3 large implies large local spectral radius of M on A, which implies small edge expansion of A in J(n,j) (Courant-Fischer). Connects our energy functional to the expansion language of the literature.
nodes.notes: PACKET WRITTEN (wave 1): full proofs + verifier 167/167 green in-branch — the walk identity, spectral cap, and phi(A) <= 1 - E_3(A)/mu(A).

nodes.title: THE ENGINE: Khot-Minzer-Safra Johnson-graph structure theorem (import)
nodes.statement: KMS/DKKMS (proved, 2018-19; the 2-to-2 games engine): non-expanding small sets in Johnson graphs correlate with JUNTAS — sets determined by membership of a bounded core of coordinates. Import note + bridge-ledger row + convention matching (their J(n,j), our exchange graph — same object). The XR inverse's stage-2 engine, now NAMED and off-the-shelf.
nodes.notes: the tightest literature fit in the program: Johnson graphs are literally KMS's domain; j/n ~ 0.4-0.5 within their regime

nodes.title: Bridge 2: juntas = fixed-core families = tangent/common-divisor structures = PAID
nodes.statement: A junta on J(n,j) (membership determined by a bounded core C of domain points) is a union of fixed-core/fixed-hole cells; correlation with a junta transfers to correlation with common-divisor (tangent-type, depth |C|) structure — landing exactly in the paid taxonomy. The k=1 dictator extremals (#191, proved) are the base case.
nodes.notes: PACKET WRITTEN (wave 1, same note): <= 2^d cell decomposition, exact divisor/avoidance characterization, factorization onto the complementary instance, 2^-d pigeonhole transfer.

nodes.title: THE RESIDUE: quantitative regime matching at FM-scale densities
nodes.statement: RESTRUCTURED: primary route = xr_globalness_from_ledger (the proved tangent ledger bounds link densities post-strip) + xr_small_set_engine (KLLM: global sets expand, losses poly in link parameters not 1/mu) + elementary composition arithmetic. The raw-KMS-constants check remains the cheap first look (the loss-exponent table), but the globalness route is expected to carry the FM-scale regime. If both compose, the XR wall's skeleton is an assembly of proved pieces plus one import. COMPOSITION TENSION (#211/E20): raw KMS/DKKMS do not provide a q-power FM engine as stated (verdict per the loss tables); KLLM's extracted stay-probability arithmetic consumes globalness beta <= q^-g and yields q^{-(g+t-1)/4} — a Q-POWER globalness demand, while the proved top-core cap gives poly(n)-scale. THE open arithmetic: does poly-globalness + the q-power pair ledger compose to n^B * FM (the KLLM engine used only for the structured residue), or does the cap need a q-power strengthening (a new, harder statement)? This is now the central question of the QX.14/QX.15 assembly.
nodes.attack_surface: parameter tables first (S-sized): tabulate the KMS/KLLM loss exponents against the FM gap per rate; if the exponents eat the gap, the KLLM global-hypercontractivity route is the fallback with sharper small-set constants
nodes.falsifier: the tables showing BOTH known quantitative forms lose more than the FM gap provides — would mean the import needs strengthening, not that the route is dead
nodes.notes: Globalness half PROOF-PINNED (see xr_globalness_from_ledger): the crux-1 conversion now has NO open conjecture of our own on the primary path — remaining: KLLM constant verification (QX.12), the E3-stability plumbing (wave-1 bridge packet), and the QX.14/QX.15 assembly. | Fleet A M3: the import skeleton encodes the decisive structural check — the chain is satisfiable ONLY IF the KLLM losses are mu-free AND the globalness definition is absolute (g3 + g1*g6 = 0). The eventual PDF verification is now a TWO-ITEM check (C2, C9), not a paper-wide audit. | FLIP on replay (Codex rednode wave 2): TARGET -> CONDITIONAL; packet in nodes/xr_kms_parameter_matching/. | REGRESSED PROVABLE -> CONDITIONAL: its predicate xr_globalness_from_ledger was honestly downgraded in wave 4; the discharge un-fires. (Policy note: downgrades propagate — auto-discharged nodes regress when a predicate leaves green.)

nodes.title: THE SHORTCUT: the proved tangent ledger IS a globalness certificate
nodes.statement: THEOREM (proof written and verified; PROVED modulo the tangent-strip normal form): if every unpaid (j-1)-core has at most L_tan aligned completions (the top-core cap — which IS the tangent/moving-root ledger: completions of a core share the degree-(j-1) divisor; #152 proves the t=2 case as the residual one-exchange-edge bound), then by an exact double count, EVERY r-core link of the post-strip family has density <= L_tan/(n-j+1), uniformly in r <= j-1. Hence A_post is (a, L_tan/(n-j+1))-global in the uniform-slice KLLM sense for all a <= j-1 — a polynomial-scale link parameter, INDEPENDENT of the FM density. The KLLM loss is then poly(n), and the q-power savings remain with the pair ledger c(s,t) = min(s,t-1): the globalness branch no longer consumes the q-budget crux 1 needs. L_tan = 1 (first-unpaid-support convention) or 2 (residual-edge convention).
nodes.attack_surface: quantify the ledger's per-cell caps as link-density bounds at each core size r; compare against KLLM's globalness threshold (the composition arithmetic); the strip handles the quotient links
nodes.falsifier: a post-strip alignment set with link density above the paid bound at some core size (would be an unpaid tangent leak — an R2 problem, not a route problem)
nodes.notes: PROVENANCE: proof by GPT Pro (relayed by project owner, 2026-07-03); VERIFIED here — algebra re-derived by hand (the binomial cancellation is exact) + 400 brute-force trials on J(10,5)/J(9,4) across all core sizes and both L_tan values. TWO FLAGS for the packet: (a) the quoted KLLM constants (delta = 10^-3 r^-1 eta^3 etc.) are [CITATION NEEDED] — verify against the actual paper in QX.12 before citing (GPT Pro hallucinated a citation once already); (b) the uniform-slice (Johnson) variant of global hypercontractivity must be cited precisely — the cube version does not transfer verbatim. FALSIFIER unchanged and sharper: an unpaid post-strip (j-1)-core with > L_tan completions = an unpaid tangent leak (E19 measures exactly this). | E19 STRESS (#209): 10 post-strip paid-tangent LINK-LEAK CANDIDATES at the exact n=16 corpus — the 'modulo strip normal form' caveat is now LOAD-BEARING: each candidate must be adjudicated (strippable under the correct L_tan convention vs a genuine unpaid tangent leak). The double-count mathematics is unaffected; the hypothesis-pinning is the open work. The #209 corpus is the adjudication test bed. | PACKET AT REPO STANDARD (Fleet A M2): G1 proved with Steiner-system tightness (Fano/SQS(8) meet the bound exactly); BONUS G2 (hereditary cap on junta cells — connects directly to the qx6_qx8 pigeonhole) and G3 (LEAK LOCALIZATION: any r-level link leak localizes upward to a top core — the #209 adjudication reduces to top-core counts only, and E19's per-r tables collapse to one level). HONEST SCOPING SHARPENED: the cap HYPOTHESIS for post-strip families at general in-band t is open — grounded only at t=2 (#152); Convention S needs an unwritten per-core stripping ledger; Convention E is #152's shape. The hypothesis at scale = exchange_ledger_gen_t territory. | C-1 ADJUDICATION (#43, replayed green, PROVED for the exact #209 corpus): all ten post-strip link-leak candidates are STRIPPABLE at L_tan = 1 — every residual support has (j-1)-core multiplicity 1 after T2-paid charging; two candidates are convention-sensitive (character pairs 9/13 and 10/14: they need the L_tan = 1 stripping convention, not L_tan = 2). The E19 leak question is CLOSED at the corpus level: the top-core cap hypothesis survives its sharpest empirical test, with the convention pinned (Convention S, L_tan = 1) as the load-bearing choice. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 4): PROVABLE -> CONDITIONAL. | WIRING FIX (amber-leaf audit, user catch #5): packet predicate exchange_ledger_gen_t was not wired req — promoted.

nodes.title: Import: global hypercontractivity (KLLM) — global sets expand, at vanishing measure
nodes.statement: Keevash-Lifshitz-Long-Minzer global hypercontractivity (proved): sets with no density boost on any small link expand almost perfectly, with losses polynomial in the link parameters rather than in 1/mu — the density-robust engine for our FM-scale (mu ~ q^{1-t}) regime, replacing raw KMS constants where they degrade. Import note + bridge-ledger row + statement matching.
nodes.notes: FLIP on replay (consolidation agent): PROVABLE -> PROVED (citation-import (KLLM hypercontractivity); scope note: does NOT close the XR wall).

nodes.title: Circuits are non-generic: the two-identity syzygy kernel vanishes off a determinantal locus
nodes.statement: The proved circuit lemma (#185) says a dependency = TWO simultaneous zero-polynomial identities. By degree counting, the simultaneous-syzygy kernel is generically zero; full-support minimal circuits exist only on an EXPLICIT determinantal locus of configurations (the two kernels intersecting in exactly dimension 1). Circuits are exceptional by construction, not just empirically.
nodes.notes: CAVEAT flagged: if the identities involve (u,v) (not just locator data), the locus lives in (u,v)-space — same argument, different variables; the E13 packet's exact lemma statement settles which

nodes.title: Schwartz-Zippel on the locus: circuit-carrying configurations are q-power suppressed
nodes.statement: The determinantal locus is cut by explicit determinants of locator-coefficient matrices of bounded degree; Schwartz-Zippel gives density <= deg/q^c per configuration family. Combined with combinatorial support counting: total circuit count <= poly(n) x (configurations / q^c) — the q-suppression that makes the branch FM-compatible.

nodes.title: Pricing: each minimal circuit contributes O(1) slopes via the deficiency-one machinery (direct reuse)
nodes.statement: A minimal circuit is a ONE-DIMENSIONAL kernel with independent deletions — exactly the deficiency-1 situation. The U-ladder's Cramer/eliminant dichotomy (u1/u2, in flight in #199) applies verbatim: each circuit yields at most O(1) unpaid slopes (eliminant branch) or lands in a named paid/structured branch (identically-valid branch). The M5 ladder built for A=384 prices the spread exceptions too — direct machinery reuse across walls.

nodes.title: The XR final assembly: three tools partition the distance scales
nodes.statement: For a fixed post-strip pair, bound |A| by the exhaustive per-scale split: (i) CLOSE pairs (exchange distance <= ledger reach s_L): residual systems suppress at a q-FACTOR per step (#152's mechanism); (ii) FAR-ONLY sets (distance-s independent): Delsarte/anticode bounds at (j/n) per step; (iii) STRUCTURED mass (link-dense): the KMS/globalness route (juntas = tangent-paid). The wall closes iff the three coverages overlap — determined by xr_radius_arithmetic. The dichotomy is exhaustive by construction; the assembly is the wall.
nodes.notes: Integrated from Codex (referee pass): status reconciled to PROVABLE (artifacts + all-green predicates); the globalness edge promoted ev -> req per the sketch's own text ('a required input, not merely evidence').

nodes.title: Each residual constraint is worth a q-factor: the ledger's exchange rate over combinatorics
nodes.statement: RESOLVED IN CLOSED FORM: c(s,t) = min(s, t-1). Theorem (proof written, verified): for agreement supports S,T of size k+t at exchange distance s, codim(K_S cap K_T) = t + min(s,t) — Case r >= k: the two degree-<k explanations are FORCED EQUAL by interpolation on the overlap, giving q^{-(t+s)}; Case r < k: the pair count q^{2k-r} gives exactly q^{-2t} (statistical independence — the plateau). All-slope accounting: same-slope branch q^{1-t-min(s,t)} (union over q slopes), distinct-slope branch q^{2-2t} (the change of variables (u,v) -> (U_z, U_z') is invertible, so distinct-slope words are independent). Net suppression relative to FM: q^{-min(s,t-1)}. LINEAR GROWTH, NO RANK COLLAPSE; saturation at t-1 is the unavoidable distinct-slope independence, not a degeneration. Base case c(1,2)=1 = #152.
nodes.notes: PROVENANCE: derived externally (GPT Pro, relayed by project owner 2026-07-03); INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED by the roadmap lane — both codim cases re-derived by hand, Monte Carlo at q=5,k=2,t=2 matches (s=1: ratio 0.98 vs q^-3; plateau: 1.07-1.11 vs q^-4). CAVEATS for the packet write-up: (a) this is the exact PAIR-CORRELATION (moment-level) ledger — the fixed-word worst-case conversion remains with the KMS/globalness branch, as the derivation itself states; (b) stated in the plain support-collinearity model — the bridge to LD_sw split-locator conventions needs its ledger row; (c) the cited 'average-support-collinearity packet' does not exist under that name — cite m1_support_coefficient_test.md and the actual #152/#193 anchors. | Fleet A M1: c(s,t) = min(s,t-1) now at repo standard — 164 exact checks over six fields incl. full word enumeration; bridge row written (forward-only per the LD_sw separation); a task-spec arithmetic bug caught and proven as a PASS check. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 4): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: The coverage table: compute the REQUIRED ledger reach s*(rate) — a number, not a hope
nodes.statement: FULL VERDICT (wave 1, 215/215 checks — CORRECTING the premature 's*=1' reading): s* = 1 NEVER closes a corridor row. Under the pinned-ledger two-regime model every corridor edge needs full reach s* = t*-1 (~8.6e9 exchange steps at rate-1/2 prize scale, ~n*2^-8); Chebyshev-strict reads identically; even the generous three-regime scenario needs s* ~ t*/15 at prize scale. THE RESIDUAL-LEDGER CONVERSION PATH IS HARD, localized to the mid band s in (t/15, t-1]. Structural facts that survive: the distinct-slope plateau = E[X]^2 exactly (Vandermonde); s=1 dominates the head; a second bulk peak (~j(n-j)/n) rides the plateau at a q^-1 discount. MITIGATIONS: the A=265 stripped exemplar needs only s* = 4 (small-t partial XR = what P2 needs); at rates 1/4, 1/8, 1/16 the corridor edges have E[X] < 1 (moment-level Markov-trivial) — THE CONTENTFUL ROWS ARE RATE 1/2 ONLY; the n^3-budget and plateau-absorption mechanisms hand off with ~3 bits of margin at the rate-1/2 edge (the arithmetic shadow of E[X] ~ B*).
nodes.notes: UNBLOCKED: c(s,t) = min(s,t-1) pinned — QX.14 is RUNNABLE NOW. Ledger reach resolved in principle: s_L = t-1 (full linear reach to the independence plateau); QX.14 determines the much smaller s* the coverage actually needs. | Ball profile N_s = C(j,s)C(n-j,s) proved + exhaustively verified; bulk peak formula verified exactly. | FLIP on replay (consolidation agent, verifier replayed): PROVABLE -> PROVED (reproduces QX.14/15 exactly; s* = t*-1 under the pinned anchored ledger convention).

nodes.title: The profile-covering obstruction: why naive generator designs cap (explains E12)
nodes.statement: A generator certificate requires the difference's 64-dim conjugate profile to match some gamma_i's profile within a factor-p^{1/phi} window per coordinate; covering arbitrary profiles needs exponentially many generators, and the e1 image is too sparse (2^122 values in a 2^390-point ball) for averaging to find full boxes. Hence: no unstructured family works, pigeonhole existence fails, and E12's linear cap is the observed shadow. The one FREE profile class: flat — (integer) x (small element) differences are self-certified.
nodes.notes: the scoping result that redirects the design search toward engineered (flat/near-integer) difference profiles

nodes.title: REFRAMING: e1 is linear — collisions = sparse ternary vectors in ONE explicit index-p lattice
nodes.statement: Since p = 1 mod N', zeta lives in F_p and e1 is an F_p-linear functional on class indicators: a collision is EXACTLY a ternary vector (entries -1/0/1, support <= 2l') in the kernel lattice K_p = {v : sum v_x zeta^x = 0 mod p}, beyond the known cyclotomic relations. The entire per-prime certification crux is a sparse-short-vector question about one explicit rank-N' lattice. Gaussian-heuristic arithmetic independently reproduces the typicality prediction (~2^-50 expected hits at N'=128).
nodes.notes: converts the crux from additive-combinatorics existence into lattice technology: search = LLL/BKZ; certification = cone-restricted dual/transference bounds (honestly harder than plain lambda_1) | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 5): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: The certificate form: cone-restricted dual bounds exclude sparse ternary kernel vectors per row
nodes.statement: For a knife-edge row's specific p: a printed lattice certificate (dual/transference bound restricted to the sparse ternary cone, or exhaustion over reduced bases) proving K_p contains no ternary vector of support <= 2l' beyond the cyclotomic relations — certifying FULL injectivity in one per-row computation. Replaces the generator-design existence question as certified_valueset_lower's primary route; fits the repo's certificate grammar (the knife-edge certificate becomes a lattice certificate).

E24's BKZ hunt is the search-side complement throughout.

The Reading-B anchor works end to end at toy scale. | FLIP on replay (Codex critical-walk batch): assembly/conditional packet in nodes/lattice_cone_certificate/conditional.md.

nodes.title: The multiplier lemma: a good c converts the mod-p relation into an EXACT integer relation
nodes.statement: For any c: if the reduced residues r_x = (c zeta^x mod+- p) satisfy sum over any support-(2l') ternary combo |sum v_x r_x| < p, then kernel membership forces the exact integer relation sum v_x r_x = 0. Elementary; the bridge from modular to integer arithmetic that every certificate below uses.
nodes.notes: True but VACUOUS at all relevant scales (Theorem 2: hypothesis unsatisfiable — zero good multipliers). Retained as scoping only. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 5): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.statement: SIMPLIFIED (post-impossibility): direct meet-in-the-middle MOD P needs NO multiplier — enumerate signed sparse sums of zeta-powers directly, check none vanish beyond the cyclotomic relations; cost ~C(N', w/2) 2^{w/2}, feasible to w ~ 24-30 at N' = 128. Extends the certified radius per row with a printed certificate.

nodes.title: k multipliers => collisions lie in the ternary kernel of an EXPLICIT k x N' integer matrix (GV-favorable at k ~ 10)

Additionally (Theorem 2, by exhaustion): zero good multipliers exist at every tested (N', p), with best-achievable residues far above threshold. nodes.title: COUNTEREXAMPLE (verified): quotient-pullback families give exponential sub-Johnson auxiliary lists
nodes.statement: Explicit construction (Codex; verified here by hand + numerically at F_31): petals = quotient fibers T_i = {x^ell = alpha_i}, L_D = H(X^ell) a pullback locator, W_S = G_S(X^ell) with G_S(Y) = Y H(Y) - prod_{i in S}(Y - alpha_i) (leading terms cancel): each W_S agrees with U* on exactly (m+1) ell = d + ell points; C(M, m+1) distinct members; sub-Johnson whenever (m+1)^2 < Mm. The unqualified correlated-target hope is FALSE: shared L_D alone cannot give poly wide lists. The family is PURE PULLBACK — chargeable to the quotient-profile budget; at the L1 cutoff M = O(log n), so the mass is poly-but-budgeted (it consumes exponent, realizing the conj:B profile term concretely).

nodes.title: DISCOVERY (X-2 counterexample, verified): the reciprocal/dihedral quotient class
nodes.statement: The palindromic flats P_m = {p : p(X) = X^{2m} p(X^{-1})} (dim m+1) are gcd-trivial, NOT contained in any multiplicative pullback g(X^M), yet contain C((n-2)/2, m) members of D_j (products of inverse-pair quadratics X^2 - (a+a^{-1})X + 1), carry one weight-2 dual word per inverse pair, and their sparse-support closed-set lattice has size >= sum_{h<=m} C((n-2)/2, h) — super-polynomial for m = omega(1). Structure: W_m = X^m F[X + X^{-1}]_{<=m} — the DIHEDRAL/CHEBYSHEV quotient x ~ x^{-1}, with the trace matroid = rank-(m+1) MDS in y = x + x^{-1}. CONVERGENCE: this is exactly Paper D v12's 'Chebyshev case' (twin cosets, K_c kernels, chi-fibers) — the cap-side machinery already treats this class; the F-taxonomy and the paid-quotient definition must enlarge to X^e g(X^M + X^{-M}). Positive residue (also verified): for FIXED dimension d the closed-set count is <= n^{O(d)} (rank bound) — extending the proved range to every fixed d with d-dependent exponent.
nodes.notes: PROVENANCE: GPT Pro X-2 verdict (outcome c — the requested counterexample branch), verified here step by step. Corrected clause: lambda_a = -+a^{-j+2m} lambda_{a^-1}. ALSO: the class is RARE among generic flats (~1/500) but dominant where reciprocal structure is built in; D_j counts are monotone in minimal dual weight; dim-3 lattices all in [284,698] (the fixed-d bound is slack — structure shrinks lattices). No fifth shape observed. | E25 VERDICT (#215, replayed green): dihedral alignments are REAL (100/64 dihedral-charged events at F_17/F_97 toys) and FULLY ABSORBED — unpaid = 0 after tangent/quotient/dihedral charges at both primes. nodes.title: REQUIRED LEMMA (QA21-G1): the per-pair dihedral-aligned support cap
nodes.statement: For a fixed pair (u,v), bound the number of simultaneously-aligned inversion-closed supports: the dihedral analogue of the tangent staircase. QA.21 shows the crude bound (all N_dih = 2 C((n-2)/2, (j-1)/2) supports) blows the clean-rate budget by +52..+292 bits (Row C) — the allowance survives only if per-pair dihedral simultaneity is suppressed by those factors. STRUCTURAL HANDLES: (a) every odd-j inversion-closed support contains one of exactly TWO points ({+1, -1}) — all dihedral-aligned supports pass through one of two links, so the top-core/link machinery bites maximally; (b) palindromic agreement rigidity (the aligned word's symmetric part is constrained); (c) E25's toy data shows small per-pair counts empirically (100/64 charged events across the whole corpus). Expected shape: linear-in-n like the tangent staircase.
nodes.attack_surface: start from the fixed-point observation: condition on the support containing +1; the aligned family through one point at one slope is a moving-root-type family — the tangent staircase argument should transfer nearly verbatim; E25's charging machinery is the toy calibration
nodes.falsifier: a toy pair aligning super-linear-in-n dihedral supports (searchable with E25's machinery)
nodes.notes: GPT PRO X-3 + Codex integration (verifier 36/36): PROVED in the deep regime 3j <= n-k — at most n+2 exact supports across ALL slopes, dihedrality not even needed (two supports pin (U,V); third codewords forced to the pencil; error-word zero sets change at <= n slopes). HONESTLY SCOPED: not applicable at the clean-rate candidates (j ~ 957 of 1024). The corridor obligation is REFRAMED by the poly-forcing compiler: the dihedral column folds into R_post <= 16 n^3 — no separate budget subtraction; this node's corridor content merges into the face-4 rungs. | POST-COMPILER ROLE: demoted req -> ev. Dihedral supports count inside R_post like any others, so the rungs' analysis covers them; this lemma is now a sharpening (and its deep-regime instance a proved template), not a gate. | FLIP on replay (Codex rednode wave 2): TARGET -> PROVED; packet in nodes/dihedral_staircase/.

nodes.title: FACE-4 LEG 1 (from E27): the pencil-cascade forcing lemma
nodes.statement: If a pair (u,v) has two aligned supports (distinct slopes) whose agreement sets share a common core of size >= k+t-1 = A-1, then the slope pencil is FORCED on the core (interpolation: core >= k determines both explaining codewords, the 2x2 slope system inverts), and the alignment CASCADES: every off-core point upgrades one further slope, so the pair's multiplicity is ~n-core and the pair is a TANGENT-PENCIL pair — paid. E27 verified every observed cascade algebraically at RS[F_97, mu_16, k=8] (the multiplicity-2 stratum has a -8.8 sigma HOLE at cores >= A-1: big-core mult-2 events are impossible, exactly as the lemma predicts). The argument is interpolation + the two-slope trick — scale-free.
nodes.attack_surface: specialize two_slope_intersection to cores >= k+t-1; the cascade step is one line (pencil evaluation off-core); write-up is a small packet
nodes.falsifier: a toy multiplicity-2 pair with core >= A-1 and no pencil (E27's machinery searches this directly; zero found in 2000 constructed + 10^5 organic)
nodes.notes: PROVED (W1, PR #10, 70/70 replayed): the forcing lemma at core >= k+t-1 with the cascade, scale-free, E27-calibrated. Face 4 rung 1 closed.

Families: slope-limited spread / engineered dimension-cap / strip-integrity. | SURVIVAL +4 (2026-07-07, Pro floor-window 5): genuine Pro attack on the shared-syzygy surface incl. k >= 4 — dependencies collapse into Pi_S(v)=0 invalid columns (live supports <= 3); no kill. nodes.title: RUNG 2a: same-slope families ARE the list challenge's object
nodes.statement: Within one slope z, distinct aligned supports = distinct codewords at distance exactly j from u+zv, and their supports are AUTOMATICALLY far-spread (two distinct codewords agree < k, so exact agreement sets of the same word overlap r < k — qx13 Theorem 1's forced-equality case, contrapositive). So the same-slope leg of xr_smallcore_spread_count is EXACTLY a worst-word list count at radius j — the SECOND grand challenge's kernel object. THE TWO GRAND CHALLENGES MEET HERE: any per-word poly list bound at the corridor radius (the list lane's own safe-side content, with its planted-sunflower machinery, petal ledgers, and clean margins at the clean rates) plugs in directly, summed over the <= q slopes with the FM/budget arithmetic controlling the slope sum.

The list-lane obligation itself lives in the list nodes, as designed. | X-4 CORRECTION: the consumed object is the SPLIT form (staircase terms charged + primitive bound), not the unqualified ExactList. Slope/support distinction limits MCA-side impact: staircase supports share slopes, so R_post's slope count survives; the spread-count support analysis charges staircase supports to the (enlarged, tail-form) quotient strip.

nodes.title: 2c-alpha (PROVABLE, 4-line proof sketched): stagnation forces a k+1 overlap budget
nodes.statement: Alignment functionals of support T are exactly the dual codewords supported in T (Lambda_T, dim t; C-perp is MDS with min weight k+1); condition rows have tensor form (lambda, z lambda). Rank stagnation at a new support forces a SYZYGY: dual codewords lambda_i in Lambda_{T_i} with sum (z_i - z_m) phi_i = 0 (the twist is invertible since slopes are distinct). Support cancellation + the MDS dual weight then force: for some member i0, supp(lambda_i0) lies inside the union of its pairwise intersections, hence sum over partners |T_i0 cap T_i'| >= k+1. QUANTITATIVE CONSEQUENCE: in the far-spread regime (each overlap < k), every stagnation event needs >= 2 partners; the minimal configuration is a TRIPLE; and stagnation events are rationed by the family's total overlap mass.
nodes.attack_surface: the derivation is complete as sketched (subtract z_m x first component; support cancellation; MDS dual weight); write the packet with the twist bookkeeping and the necessity/sufficiency gap stated (the twisted-psi membership in Lambda_{T_m} is an ADDITIONAL constraint beyond the syzygy)
nodes.falsifier: a toy stagnation with overlap budget < k+1 (mechanical check in any stacked-rank scan)
nodes.notes: PROVED (23,480 checks): the k+1 overlap budget, in the rank-linear-algebra model of qx13 (the ledger's own model — scoping consistent across the whole chain).

nodes.title: 2c-gamma: diffuse budgets — scattered syzygies ARE face 2's lattice objects
nodes.statement: When the k+1 budget is spread over many small overlaps: the syzygy's dual codeword has SCATTERED support exactly covered by the intersection pattern — and counting constrained-support dual codewords and their linear relations is precisely Conjecture F's sparse-dual/closed-set lattice object. FACE 4's core meets FACE 2 here: the diffuse-stagnation count is an F-lattice count over the intersection-pattern flats. With faces 3+4 already sharing the spread residue and 2a consuming the list lane, ALL FOUR kernel faces are now pairwise connected through concrete shared objects — the kernel is one problem in the strongest possible sense. DECOMPOSED: gamma-a the transport dictionary (PROVABLE); gamma-b bounded syzygy dimension — the PROVED fixed-d lattice bound + the four-branch strip apply (proved reduction); gamma-c growing dimension — possible only when total overlap exceeds (m-1)k, and that regime IS face 2's f_many_sparse_structure (an identification, not new content: the F-residue and face 4's diffuse residue are the same node).
nodes.attack_surface: transport the F-descent machinery (four-branch strip incl. dihedral, fixed-d lattice bounds, E30's small-lattice data) to the intersection-pattern flats; the scattered supports have bounded piece sizes (< k), which is exactly the regime where descent behaved polynomially at toys

nodes.title: beta-1 (PROVABLE, proof complete): sunflowers never stagnate — the 2n/t cap
nodes.statement: If an aligned far-spread family is a sunflower (all pairwise intersections equal one common core Y, |Y| < k), every syzygy member is supported inside Y (cancellation only on shared points), and |Y| < k+1 = the MDS dual minimum weight forces all members to vanish: NO nontrivial syzygy exists, so by the alpha anatomy every member contributes its FULL t rows and the family size is <= 2n/t per pair (~630 at prize-max rate 1/4). REVERSAL NOTE: the list lane's planted-sunflower worst cases are exactly the MCA-side's provably-safe configurations — the two challenges' hard directions are disjoint here.

nodes.notes: PROVED (552,814 checks): sunflowers never stagnate; the exact cap m <= floor(2n/t) per pair is proved, not just the shape.

nodes.title: FACE-2 KEYSTONE (PROVABLE): the quotient sector is complete by group theory
nodes.statement: Quotient-type escape mechanisms arise from Mobius symmetries of the evaluation domain; the PGL_2 stabilizer of mu_n (and of 2-power coset domains) contains the dihedral group Dih_n (rotations x -> zeta x, inversions x -> zeta/x) and generically equals it. Every subgroup of a dihedral group is cyclic or dihedral (theorem): cyclic => multiplicative pullbacks g(X^M); dihedral => Chebyshev quotients X^e g(X^M + zeta' X^-M) (twisted inversions are conjugate relabelings). THEREFORE the symmetry-quotient taxonomy {multiplicative, dihedral} is COMPLETE — no sixth quotient mechanism exists. Explains E25/E27/E30's clean fifth-shape watches structurally. Residual check (small lemma): no exotic stabilizer at special (n, q) — the stabilizer of an n-point projective set with n >= 3 is finite and contains Dih_n; rule out proper overgroups for prize domains. AMENDED (Codex P2 + verified negative controls + the Mersenne analysis): the raw all-(n,q) claim is FALSE. TAME rows: stabilizer = Dih_n PROVED (P2's tame case). WILD rows — the stabilizer explodes to PGL_2(F_{n-1}) (3-transitive, order ~n^3) exactly when the domain is projectively the subfield line P^1(F_{n-1}): requires n-1 a prime power AND F_{n-1} in F_q. For 2-power n, n-1 prime power <=> Mersenne structure: n in {4, 8, 32, 128, 2^13, 2^17, 2^19, 2^31} (exhaustive to 2^41; both negative controls verified independently: F_9/mu_4 -> 24 = S_4 by my brute force, F_49/mu_8 -> 336 = |PGL_2(F_7)|). ADMISSIBLE WILD ROWS EXIST: q = (2^m - 1)^{2s} < 2^256 with n | q - 1 automatic (q'^2 - 1 = (q'-1) 2^m for Mersenne q') at n = 2^13, 2^17, 2^19, 2^31. Row C (1023 = 3*11*31) and prize-max (2^41 - 1 composite) are TAME. At wild rows the completeness conclusion enlarges from {cyclic, dihedral} to the DICKSON subgroup lattice of PGL_2(F_{n-1}) — still finite and classical. Named condition: p2_no_wild_subfield_circle_domain (per-row tameness check, pure arithmetic).
nodes.attack_surface: the subgroup theorem is classical; the stabilizer computation is a short PGL_2 argument; the exotic-case check is finite casework (n = 2-power, n | q-1)

The 'explains the clean fifth-shape watches' note is retracted: those censuses were weight-<=4-blind.

nodes.title: FACE-2 CORE (evidence-informed): the weight-2 inverse theorem
nodes.statement: RESTATED ALONG E35's OBSERVED STRUCTURE (1452 flats, 28/28): if a flat (post common-root strip) carries >= 4 DISJOINT minimal weight-2 dual supports (the MATCHING count, not raw pairs — near-pencils reach raw 6 without symmetry but matching 2), then it is contained in a projective-multiplicative stratum X^e g(X^M) or a dihedral stratum. Empirical basis: the distribution has an EMPTY BAND — nonsymmetric flats have matching count 0-3, symmetric 7-8, nothing at 4-6 (threshold 4, margin 3); collapse under perturbation is all-or-nothing (7-8 -> <=2 in one cell), so the inverse theorem is sharp with no soft regime. PROOF PLAN (two steps): (i) matching composition — >= 4 disjoint two-point ratio constraints compose into a global symmetry candidate; (ii) the candidate lands in the FINITE target (f_dih_subgroup_completeness: cyclic or dihedral, nothing else). STEP (i) PROVED AT QUADRATIC THRESHOLD (Theorem 1, verifier 7/7: m > j + (j-1)^2 disjoint weight-2 supports force GLOBAL PULLBACK: Phi = nu compose psi, every psi-fiber pair a flat-wide ratio edge; engine = value rigidity + P1xP1 Bezout + Luroth; linear threshold W(d) = d+2 open with Lemma D as seed). STEP (ii) FALSIFIED AS STATED and CORRECTED: the conclusion space is NOT Dih_n — witness span{1, q, q^2}, q = X^2+3X over F_17: post-strip, w2m = 7, no Dih stratum, psi-fibers of the affine involution x -> -3-x. The TRUE conclusion space is the LUROTH/PULLBACK LATTICE (intermediate fields F(psi) of F(X)), of which the Dih_n-invariant subfields are the group special case — completeness now by LUROTH'S THEOREM (every intermediate field is F(psi)): closure by classification at the correct level. E35's empty band survives with the enlarged conclusion: abundant flats are pullback-structured (symmetric OR not); primitive stay <= 3.
nodes.attack_surface: step (i) is the content: disjoint weight-2 words are ratio-edges on the domain; composition/transitivity across 4+ disjoint edges within a d-dim flat forces the edge map to extend (dimension count: each independent edge cuts dim by 1; 4 edges over dim <= 3 over-determine); step (ii) is the keystone; E35's all-or-nothing collapse says no stability epsilon is needed

nodes.notes: E35's two LEDGER CORRECTIONS (falsifier-grade, no S9): (1) the multiplicative clause must be PROJECTIVE — X^e g(X^M), not bare g(X^M) ({X,X^3,X^5} has 8 weight-2 supports and is a monomial-twisted pullback the literal list misses; same X^e prefix shape as the dihedral clause); (2) abundance = disjoint-support matching count. Bonus: odd-parity maximal dihedral strata carry forced weight-1 words (fixed-point ratio product -1) — they sit BEHIND the tangent strip already. | Named gaps (alpha, beta, gamma) recorded in f_matching_composition.md; sharpness data: non-Dih involutions attain at most 7 pairs at n = 16 (160 attainers), only Dih reach 8.

E29's measurement then calibrates constants instead of deciding existence. AMENDED PER E34 (15/15, 519 cells): replace R by its congruence-class CLOSURE mod D = gcd(M, {r - r'}): the joint stabilizer is K_D, all of R lies in one class r0 mod D, and the joint-stabilizer subcode is a SINGLE character-r0 isotypic instance at scale D (Lemma J1); joint data is contained in telescoped data always, with EQUALITY iff |R| = M/D (Lemma J2). Census: containment in all 519 cells (falsifier never fired); equality on all 343 full-class cells — the q^{M-1} over-aggregation vanishes BY CONSTRUCTION. E29 demotes to constants calibration.

nodes.title: THE FIFTH MECHANISM (X-4b verdict, VERIFIED): primitive moment-trade / PTE staircases
nodes.statement: Cancellation is NOT symmetry: any disjoint family of t-moment-null blocks (e_1(B) = ... = e_t(B) = 0, |B| > t) generates exact-list staircases exactly as the quotient/dihedral ones do — the block locators have zero top-t sub-leading coefficients, so unions share top coefficients and differences drop to degree < k. VERIFIED PRIMITIVE WITNESS: B = {11^e : e in {0,1,2,4,16,45,50,60}} in mu_64 over F_193 — power sums vanish for r = 1,2,3 (r = 4 gives 18), locator top-3 sub-leading coefficients literally zero, NO rotation or reflection stabilizer (all independently recomputed). Replicated across cosets: post-strip exact lists >= C(R, R/2), exponential in n — the quotient/dihedral strip provably misses it. CODING IDENTITY: t-moment-null blocks are 0/1-coefficient dual codewords with t leading zero syndromes — sparse dual words at weights ABOVE every census we ran (E9/E30/E35 covered weight <= 4; structural blindness, honest lesson). The symmetry classes are the special case where invariance forces moment-nullity; the general class is Prouhet-Tarry-Escott-flavored and p-SPECIFIC.

nodes.notes: THE UNIFICATION (matching-composition witness + fiber arithmetic, verified): t-moment-null blocks of size b ARE fibers of polynomials with zero top-t coefficients (elementary symmetric functions of a fiber = the map's coefficients, invariant in the fiber value below e_b), and the matching-composition counterexample's psi-fiber pairs are the same class at b = 2. The fifth mechanism = PULLBACK ALONG RATIONAL MAPS with coefficient constraints; the symmetry quotients are its invertible/group special cases. Trade FAMILIES mix maps across blocks (each block a fiber of its own map) — the family class is the mixed-pullback closure. | FLIP on replay (Codex wave 3): PROVABLE -> PROVED.

nodes.title: U1 — THE CLOSING CONJECTURE: cancellation is pullback, quantitatively
nodes.statement: RESTATED (X-5 verdict — the tautology repaired): U1 = THE COMPRESSION THEOREM u1_pte_trade_compression. By the star-PTE lemma (PROVED), every same-top-t family is a star of canonical order-t PTE trades from any base — representation is free; the conjecture is COMPRESSION: if |F| > n^2, all but <= n^2 canonical trades belong to a FINITE CHARGEABLE PULLBACK DICTIONARY (data: finitely many rational maps psi_alpha, fiber values, fixed tails, zero-top-t coefficient constraints, and an EXACT staircase counting rule — the bounded-complexity grammar W3 must define). Falsifiable form u1_primitive_star_pte_bound: post-dictionary-strip, <= n^2 star trades survive from any base (toy harness: enumerate same-top-t families, canonical trades, filter by dictionary, count survivors). Quotient-row transport verbatim (the GAP-1 per-leaf form). EVIDENCE: six documented demolition attacks (cross-block compensation, defect-plus-point, mixed sizes, poles, towers, char-p switch nets) all die into canonical trades or rational-pullback variants — the strongest pre-proof evidence compression can have. nodes.title: PROVED (X-5): the star-PTE normal form — no non-PTE cancellation exists
nodes.statement: For any family of monic degree-A locators with roots in D sharing top t coefficients, and any base member S0: every member S decomposes canonically as S = C u P, S0 = C u Q with |P| = |Q| >= t+1 (unless S = S0) and e_r(P) = e_r(Q) for r <= t. Proof: L_S - L_S0 = L_C (L_P - L_Q); the degree constraint forces the top-t agreement; h <= t forces L_P = L_Q. Exact, scale-free, transports verbatim to quotient rows. CONSEQUENCE: there is no non-PTE cancellation mechanism at the two-locator level — any residual hardness is COMPRESSION of many trades, not representation.

nodes.title: PROVED (X-6): the characteristic-zero lifting theorem
nodes.statement: If char(F_q) = p > max_{r <= t} C(b,r)^phi(n), then every b-subset of mu_n (2-power n) with e_1 = ... = e_t = 0 is a union of mu_M-cosets, M = 2^ceil(log2(t+1)) > t — quotient-staircase charged; if M does not divide b, no such block exists. UNIFORM IN b. Proof: mod-p vanishing lifts to characteristic zero above the norm bound; char-0 e_1 = 0 with 0/1 indicator forces Phi_n | P_B hence antipodal closure B = -B; locator descends to Q_1(X^2); iterate. Verified here. The threshold (~phi(n) log2 C(b,r) bits — thousands) is far above 2^128: correct but not the requested bound.

nodes.title: INPUT T: the toral fiber-product classification
nodes.statement: deg psi < p, D = gamma mu_n, p coprime to n: if psi's non-diagonal fiber product has a toral component x^a y^b = c, or psi has > poly(deg) n^{2/3} full D-fibers, then psi is v1-chargeable (monomial/power or Dickson/Chebyshev pullback, up to linear conjugacy, coset scaling, bounded tails). STRONGER than Ritt's Second Theorem (which IS verified: tame composition collisions of coprime degrees are monomial-or-Dickson, von zur Gathen normal forms); the needed form is a Laurent/toral statement — Zieve's Laurent-polynomial decompositions are the right orbit, unverified at this exact strength. CORRECTED WORDING (per X-7): Frobenius/additive wild composition is excluded by deg < p; tame exceptional families (monomial, Dickson) are the DICTIONARY, not absent phenomena. X-8 CORRECTIONS: (i) the exact-polynomial toral case is PROVED and gives MONOMIAL ONLY (t_poly_exact_toral) — Dickson requires the Laurent/rational case, so the load-bearing missing theorem is a POSITIVE-CHARACTERISTIC LAURENT-RITT statement (named; Zannier's polynomial Ritt II does not cover it); (ii) the many-fiber threshold MUST carry the n^2/p term: the trigger is K > C(d) max{n^{2/3}, n^2/p}, equivalently the hypotheses need p >= C(d) n^{4/3} — SATISFIED at the six candidates by ~200 bits (p ~ 2^250 vs n^{4/3} = 2^54.7) but low-q admissible rows are NOT covered (the same low-q danger zone as the boundary column); (iii) the bounded-tails wrapper is a named missing lemma (fiber-minus-B-points incidence loss). T = one proved subtheorem + conditional assembly on {Laurent-Ritt, corrected threshold, tails lemma}.

THE COMPLICATION FOUND: Zieve's 'Decompositions of Laurent polynomials' (the natural one-shot import) is CHARACTERISTIC 0 (built on Bilu-Tichy) — cannot be imported directly; the route goes through Zannier + substitution instead. THE TWO SEAMS THAT ARE OURS: (a) lifting general bidegree (a,b) toral factors to the quadratic/KMS case via the substitution (elementary-looking, needs writing); (b) the bounded-tails robustness wrapper. Status upgraded TARGET -> PROVABLE: an assembly task with named sources, not an open import. | T IS NOW ASSEMBLED MODULO ONE LEMMA: toral branch PROVED (t_laurent_ritt_toral_stabilizer — cyclic/dihedral pullback exactly); many-fibers branch = the verified subgroup-point bound with the corrected max{n^{2/3}, n^2/p} threshold (satisfied at the candidates by ~200 bits) forces a toral component, then the theorem applies; REMAINING: the bounded-tails wrapper (fibers-minus-B-points incidence loss — lemma-sized, named in X-8). Status TARGET -> CONDITIONAL on the tails wrapper. GRAMMAR NOTE: v1's charged forms should read outer-LAURENT in the cyclic branch per the X-9 correction.

nodes.title: PROVED (X-8 task 2): exact polynomial toral components give power-pullback only
nodes.statement: char K = 0 or > deg psi: if psi(X) - psi(Y) has a non-diagonal toral component in (G_m)^2, the component is X = lambda Y and psi = phi(X^m) (m = ord(lambda) > 1) after scaling — the monomial case ONLY. Proof: sign/degree bookkeeping kills X^A Y^B = c (positive exponents vs negative); X^A = c Y^B forces A = B = 1; psi(lambda Y) = psi(Y) forces lambda^i = 1 on all exponents. Verified here (elementary). CONSEQUENCE: Dickson/Chebyshev does NOT arise from polynomial toral components — it needs Laurent/rational psi, so T's Laurent case is load-bearing, not decorative.

nodes.title: PROVED (X-9): the tame Laurent-Ritt / toral stabilizer theorem — T's core
nodes.statement: k algebraically closed, psi a Laurent polynomial of degree d, char 0 or p > d: if the reduced non-diagonal fiber product has a geometric toral component, then EITHER psi = F(x^m), m > 1, F LAURENT (F in k[z, 1/z]; F in k[z] if psi is an ordinary polynomial) — the cyclic branch — OR psi = F(x^m + alpha x^-m) with F in k[z] — the dihedral/Dickson branch. Converse holds. PROOF SELF-CONTAINED, ZERO IMPORTS (verified here incl. the dihedral factorization identity and the discriminant of the rational counterexample): (1) degree multiplicativity collapses every primitive toral component x^a y^b = c to y = lambda x or xy = c — the graph of a tame automorphism of G_m; (2) the toral stabilizer is finite (separability from p > d) and cyclic-or-dihedral; (3) invariant fields k(x^m) and k(x^m + alpha x^-m) + the Laurent pole argument pin the outer map. TWO WORDING CORRECTIONS to T as I posed it: the cyclic outer map is Laurent, not polynomial (witness x^m + x^-2m); general rational psi needs an outer RATIONAL map (witness R(z) = z + 1/(z^2+1), degree 3, no totally ramified fiber, discriminant 592). Scale-free; tame hypothesis survives every quotient row with ~2^100 slack.
nodes.notes: PROVENANCE: GPT Pro X-9 (10-for-10 substantive). The theorem is EASIER than full Laurent-Ritt precisely because the degree collapse kills general-bidegree components geometrically — the fence that made T look hard was an artifact of not parametrizing. | T-TOY (#32, 212/212): the classification confirmed empirically through degree 20 at tame toy rows — zero unclassified bidegree-(1,1) toral/affine cases; line factors are linear-conjugate power pullbacks; Dickson appears at the X^2 symmetry exactly as the theorem predicts.
