nodes.notes: Pro follow-up brief issued (same ef thread): the S9 typecheck — prove every trivial-stab noncontainment non-tower horizontal component is a base-list F-valued witness line (linear-pencil lever) or exhibit the S9 obstruction. Closes ef chain -> f1. | Pro round 2 (verified): linearity ALONE does not close it (S9 obstruction exhibited). Reduced to: (proved) F-valued=>e-interleaved witness + (open) interleaved_floor_import (does ext_pole_floor cover interleaved witnesses?). Ordinary-base-list version FALSE.

nodes.title: Petal residue-kernel linear bound
nodes.statement: In the L1 coset-chart full-petal residue bridge, the residue-line kernel K_{I,d}=ker(pi_{>d} R_{I,d}) satisfies the proved Lemma 13 bound dim K <= c+1, where c=d-ell.
nodes.attack_surface: none; this is the already banked L1 Lemma 13 bound made explicit for the petal branch
nodes.falsifier: a coset-chart residue instance violating dim K <= c+1
nodes.notes: AUDIT FLAG RESOLVED (verified): Lemma 13 (dim K <= c+1, i.e. rank floor r >= ell) holds PERFECTLY in-regime — verified ell=3,t=5, c=2..8: r>=ell and dim_K in {0,0,1,3,5,7,9} all <= c+1 (tight at c=8). The apparent census discrepancy was OUT OF SCOPE: the census's ell=2,t=3 has d=c+ell > (t-1)ell-1=3 for c>=2, collapsing the residue space to <ell columns so r<ell is forced by dimensions. Lemma 13 is SOUND; the l1 note (line 91) confirms the floor is never violated in-regime.

nodes.title: Petal squarefree classification counting soundness
nodes.statement: If the squarefree realizable locator points in each residue-line kernel decompose into finitely many charged classes and uncharged classes each bounded by a polynomial with exponent independent of c, then petal_kernel_realizable_sparsity holds.
nodes.attack_surface: finite-union counting rule for classified squarefree kernel points
nodes.falsifier: a finite classification with each uncharged class uniformly polynomial but whose union grows beyond the same uniform-polynomial regime

nodes.title: Petal squarefree classification-ledger soundness
nodes.statement: If a ledger covers every squarefree realizable locator point in the residue-line kernels by disjoint charged or uncharged class records, every charged record cites an already paid family, every uncharged record has a polynomial bound with exponent independent of c, and the number of uncharged records is independent of c, then petal_squarefree_kernel_classification_payload holds.
nodes.attack_surface: formal coverage and charged/uncharged dispatch for Petal squarefree classification ledgers
nodes.falsifier: a complete valid classification ledger while the squarefree-kernel classification payload fails

nodes.title: Petal realizable extras inject into residue kernel
nodes.statement: In the L1 coset-chart full-petal residue bridge, every exact realizable full-petal extra determines a squarefree locator point lying in the residue-line kernel, and this assignment is injective.
nodes.attack_surface: Lemma 8 full-petal rank certificate from the coset-chart bridge
nodes.falsifier: two distinct realizable full-petal extras with the same locator point, or a realizable extra whose locator does not lie in the residue-line kernel

nodes.title: SOV active-core obstruction vanishing
nodes.statement: Every active h-core in the intended h>20 band corresponds to a finite h-trade whose locator L satisfies the forced-root obstruction equations, so all obstruction coordinates from sov_forced_root_recursion_algebra vanish.
nodes.attack_surface: connect active-core equations to the finite h-trade square-shift model used by X79, uniformly for h in the intended band
nodes.falsifier: an active h-core whose locator is not in the square-shift trade model or has a nonzero forced-root obstruction coordinate

nodes.title: SOV first-obstruction coefficient sensitivity
nodes.statement: For the forced-root obstruction construction, changing only the coefficient of X^{h-1} in the locator L leaves the forced root unchanged and changes the first obstruction O_{h-1} by exactly the negative of that coefficient change. Hence O_{h-1} is the correct coordinate-sensitive obstruction to use for the value-set problem.
nodes.attack_surface: triangular forced-root recursion and coefficient inspection
nodes.falsifier: a unit change in the X^{h-1} coefficient that changes the forced root or fails to change O_{h-1} by -1

nodes.title: SOV forced-root recursion algebra
nodes.statement: For every h over odd characteristic, the monic degree-h square-root recursion uniquely recovers the degree-h polynomial S forced by the top h+1 coefficients of a monic degree-2h locator L, and the obstruction vector is exactly the list of coefficients of S^2-L in degrees 1 through h-1. In the X79 shifted-constant trade model the recursion returns the midpoint and all obstructions vanish.
nodes.attack_surface: symbolic triangular coefficient recursion; local verifier replays the generalized X79 gate for several h
nodes.falsifier: a degree 2h locator whose top coefficients do not determine the forced root by the printed recursion, or a shifted-constant X79 trade with a nonzero obstruction

nodes.title: SOV h-1 fiber Fourier duality
nodes.statement: For any forced-root conditioned anchored-core fiber and the map c(L)=[X^{h-1}]L to the row field, the largest fiber of c is bounded by the uniform term plus the average of the nontrivial additive character sums of c.
nodes.attack_surface: finite-field Fourier inversion on the coefficient map
nodes.falsifier: a finite map to a field whose fiber size exceeds the Fourier-inversion bound

nodes.title: SOV nonconstant affine character cancellation
nodes.statement: For any finite-dimensional affine space over the row field, any nontrivial additive character, and any affine-linear coefficient map with nonzero linear part, the nontrivial character sum over the affine space is zero. For a disjoint partition into such affine pieces plus an exceptional set, the total character sum is bounded by the exceptional-set size.
nodes.attack_surface: finite-field additive-character orthogonality on a nonconstant affine map
nodes.falsifier: a nonconstant affine-linear map over a finite field whose nontrivial additive character sum is nonzero

nodes.title: SOV forced-root obstruction correctness
nodes.statement: The generalized forced square-root recursion correctly produces the obstruction vector for every h in the intended band, and every active h-core forces the relevant obstruction coordinates to vanish.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the proved forced-root recursion algebra and the remaining active-core obstruction-vanishing theorem
nodes.falsifier: an active core in the intended h band whose forced-root obstruction vector does not vanish, or a mismatch with the banked h=5/X79 construction

nodes.title: SOV affine-piece partition certificate soundness
nodes.statement: If a certificate partitions every forced-root conditioning cell into disjoint affine pieces where c(L)=[X^{h-1}]L has nonzero linear part, plus paid/norm-structured exceptional pieces whose total size is below the declared character-sum budget, then sov_hminus1_affine_piece_decomposition holds.
nodes.attack_surface: finite partition and affine-linear certificate semantics for SOV conditioned cells
nodes.falsifier: a verified affine/exceptional partition certificate that fails to imply the SOV h-1 affine-piece decomposition

nodes.title: F-valued word source => e-interleaved base-list witness (Pro, verified)
nodes.statement: Every trivial-stabilizer noncontainment non-tower horizontal component with F-valued word source is, at its generic point, an e=[E:B]-interleaved base-field multiplication-slice witness (syndrome commutes with B-basis expansion since D subset B; mult-by-z = M_z; noncontainment preserved). The ordinary-base-list version is FALSE (proved extension-line counterexample).

nodes.title: ext_pole_floor covers e-interleaved base-list witnesses (the S9 seam)
nodes.statement: REFINED (Pro round 3, verified): the interleaved list-counting is EXACT — the e-interleaved witness has effective size L_eff = #{(A_1..A_e): |cap A_i| >= a} = the ordinary EXTENSION-CODE list size |Lambda(C_E, 1-a/n, f+zg)| (L2 bridge), NOT L^e; interleaving filters the product by common support. The pole floor then applies with N_int(L_eff) = ceil(L_eff X /(X + kappa_int L_eff)). SOLE remaining seam: ExtPoleFloorAppliesToWeilRestrictedInterleaving — the Paper D floor PROOF is functorial under Weil restriction (accepts a B-defined Weil-restricted interleaved witness as a base list). FOR THE GEOMETRIC EXCLUSION (ef_pole_free_cycle_exclusion) the SINGLETON suffices: L_eff=1 => N_int(1)=ceil(X/(X+kappa))=1 (VERIFIED, all X>0) => component meets D_E. So ef exclusion closes modulo the singleton functoriality; only the numerator LEDGER needs the full L_eff gate.
nodes.falsifier: an e-interleaved base-list witness escaping ext_pole_floor -- the S9 class.
nodes.notes: Pro follow-up brief issued (same ef thread): does N(L) apply to the interleaved witness effective list size? Prove (A) or exhibit S9 (B). The crux is pure list-counting of the multiplication-slice vs N(L). Closes ef->f1. | PRO ROUND 3 (verified): list-counting EXACT (L_eff = extension-code list, not L^e; interleaving is diagonal not Cartesian). Singleton exclusion N_int(1)=1 VERIFIED. Reduced to Weil-restriction functoriality of the Paper D floor proof (singleton for exclusion) — a scope property of an EXISTING proof, checkable by inspecting it. | PROVED (Pro W2, verified): the Paper D simple-pole floor is Weil-functorial for the singleton multiplication-slice witness (CA-bad construction verified, numerator L=1); no S9 class. Closes ef->f1.

nodes.title: DLI circle log-integral constant
nodes.statement: The exact circle constant in DLI is int_0^1 log |cos(2 pi x)|^2 dx = -2 log 2.
nodes.attack_surface: classical one-dimensional integral
nodes.falsifier: a corrected evaluation of the circle integral

nodes.title: DLI odd-phase polar-obstruction soundness
nodes.statement: If, for each actual DLI odd-evaluation phase on each relevant square-root component, a certificate identifies a pole of the Artin-Schreier-reduced phase with positive order not divisible by p, then the phase is not of Artin-Schreier form g^p-g+c.
nodes.attack_surface: formal local-pole obstruction to Artin-Schreier triviality
nodes.falsifier: a phase of Artin-Schreier form g^p-g+c whose reduced representative has a pole order prime to p

nodes.title: DLI Deligne-to-Weyl transfer
nodes.statement: If, for every central profile, nonzero frequency, and harmonic m in the DLI ranges, the odd-evaluation phase on each square-root component defines a geometrically nontrivial bounded-conductor trace sheaf whose conductor budget has harmonic total o(t), then Deligne/Weil square-root cancellation gives the finite-frequency Weyl-sum bounds required by dli_odd_eval_exponential_sum_bound.
nodes.attack_surface: standard curve/sheaf exponential-sum transfer plus harmonic summation of the conductor budget
nodes.falsifier: a geometrically nontrivial bounded-conductor odd-evaluation phase satisfying the stated conductor budget but failing square-root Weyl cancellation

nodes.title: DLI truncated-log discrepancy transfer
nodes.statement: If the odd-evaluation sequence satisfies the stated truncated-log discrepancy bounds for the Dirichlet kernel and the stated peak-mass tail bounds near its singular peaks, with total error sum_j eps_j=o(t), then the DLI odd-evaluation discrepancy predicate follows.
nodes.attack_surface: deterministic truncation/layer-cake transfer from peak discrepancy to the logarithmic geometric-mean bound
nodes.falsifier: a sequence satisfying the truncated-log and peak-mass hypotheses but failing the corresponding log-integral lower bound

nodes.title: DLI reduced-phase manifest soundness
nodes.statement: If a reduced-phase manifest covers every central profile, nonzero frequency, DLI harmonic, and square-root component, gives an Artin-Schreier-reduced local pole of positive order prime to p for each tuple, gives a certified polar-divisor majorant for the same tuple, and proves the harmonic majorant total is o(t), then both dli_odd_phase_polar_obstruction_payload and dli_reduced_pole_majorant_table_payload hold.
nodes.attack_surface: schema soundness tying the DLI local polar obstruction and reduced-pole majorant table to one manifest
nodes.falsifier: a verified reduced-phase manifest satisfying the pole and majorant predicates while one DLI payload fails

nodes.title: DLI reduced-pole majorant-table soundness
nodes.statement: If a table covers every central profile, nonzero frequency, DLI harmonic, and square-root component; gives a certified upper bound for the Artin-Schreier-reduced polar divisor of each covered phase; and proves that the sum of those majorants over the DLI harmonic ranges is o(t), then dli_reduced_pole_harmonic_ledger holds.
nodes.attack_surface: formal coverage, domination, and summation rule for DLI reduced-pole majorant tables
nodes.falsifier: a complete certified majorant table with total o(t) while the actual reduced-pole harmonic ledger fails

nodes.title: DLI Erdos-Turan peak-mass reduction
nodes.statement: If the normalized odd-evaluation sequence satisfies the required finite-frequency Weyl-sum bounds up to the reciprocal scales used by the DLI peak neighborhoods, then Erdos-Turan plus dyadic annuli around the Dirichlet peaks gives the truncated-log discrepancy and peak-mass tail bounds required by dli_truncated_log_transfer.
nodes.attack_surface: deterministic interval-discrepancy and dyadic layer-cake reduction
nodes.falsifier: a sequence satisfying the stated Weyl-sum bounds but violating the required peak-mass or truncated-log discrepancy bound

nodes.title: DLI Artin-Schreier conductor criterion
nodes.statement: For an additive phase f on a smooth curve component, the associated Artin-Schreier trace sheaf is geometrically trivial exactly when f differs from a constant by g^p-g; otherwise its conductor is bounded by the reduced polar divisor of f after Artin-Schreier reduction.
nodes.attack_surface: standard Artin-Schreier sheaf triviality and conductor bookkeeping
nodes.falsifier: a non-Artin-Schreier rational phase whose additive trace sheaf is geometrically trivial, or whose conductor exceeds its reduced polar-divisor bound

nodes.title: DLI odd-phase budget ledger soundness
nodes.statement: If a DLI odd-phase ledger covers every central profile, nonzero frequency, harmonic, and square-root component; marks every phase non-Artin-Schreier; and supplies reduced polar-divisor bounds whose harmonic total is o(t), then dli_odd_phase_reduced_pole_budget holds.
nodes.attack_surface: formal coverage and summation rule for DLI reduced-pole ledgers
nodes.falsifier: a complete ledger with all phases marked nontrivial and total o(t) while the reduced-pole budget statement fails

nodes.title: DLI phase-side pole+conductor manifest (Pro, verified)
nodes.statement: For each DLI tuple: the Artin-Schreier reduced phase hP_lambda has a prime-to-p pole of order ed (d odd <p, e a 2-power sqrt pullback), surviving nondegeneracy, with conductor majorant ed + C_tame. Parts 1-3 of the reduced-phase manifest.

nodes.title: M720 official norm-gate certificate soundness
nodes.statement: A complete official h=7..20 norm-gate payload, or a uniform nonvanishing theorem covering the same primitive norm-gate branch, soundly excludes all unpaid non-toral active cores in the official h=7..20 band once m720_official_paid_branch_alignment has reduced the problem to that branch.
nodes.attack_surface: formal certificate semantics and paid/norm-gate branch coverage
nodes.falsifier: a payload marked complete and zero for every primitive norm-gate case while an unpaid non-toral official h=7..20 active core survives

nodes.title: M720 calibration certificate semantics
nodes.statement: For M720 MITM outputs, a zero active-core count is a complete calibration-cell zero certificate only when the run has complete=true, equivalently W=n and the run did not abort. If complete=false, the output is slice-local evidence only and cannot be promoted to a global zero certificate.
nodes.attack_surface: formal consequence of the mitm_slice complete flag and enumerator contract
nodes.falsifier: a certificate protocol that treats W<n or aborted output as a complete global zero

nodes.title: M720 over-ceiling complete-window certificates
nodes.statement: The two over-ceiling n=32,h=16 complete-window cells at q~n^2 and q~n^3 have complete certificates with zero unpaid non-toral active-core count, or a corrected certificate policy excludes them from complete-cell use.
nodes.attack_surface: algebraic complement-pair classification at n=32,h=16
nodes.falsifier: a nonzero unpaid non-toral active core in either over-ceiling complete-window cell

nodes.title: M720 MITM correctness gate
nodes.statement: The MITM scanner used for the h in (6,20] band correctly detects known h=3,4,5 trade/census facts and distinguishes toral or paid cores from non-toral active cores.
nodes.attack_surface: connect scanner equations to the banked C1a/C1b trade equations; off-laptop gate replay is evidence only
nodes.falsifier: a known small-h trade missed by the scanner, or a paid/toral calibration core misclassified as an unpaid non-toral active core

nodes.title: M720 official norm-gate case-manifest soundness
nodes.statement: If a manifest covers every official-shape primitive h=7..20 norm-gate case and, for each case, supplies either a uniform nonvanishing theorem citation or a complete=true zero-survivor certificate record, then m720_official_h7_20_norm_gate_payload holds.
nodes.attack_surface: formal coverage and accepted-record dispatch for official M720 norm-gate manifests
nodes.falsifier: a complete valid official norm-gate manifest while the M720 payload fails

nodes.title: M720 off-laptop complete zero certificates
nodes.statement: For the h=7..20 calibration cells used by midlarge_h7_20, complete zero-certificate payloads supply cell parameters, complete=true flags, and zero unpaid non-toral active-core counts.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from WSL-safe complete-cell certificates and residual off-laptop complete-cell execution
nodes.falsifier: a complete calibration cell with nonzero unpaid non-toral active cores, or missing/false completeness metadata

nodes.title: M720 residual slice metadata
nodes.statement: Under the Modal count-ceiling window rule, every h=7..20 residual cell beyond the WSL-safe complete zero cells is either a W<n window slice or one of exactly two over-ceiling complete-window cells: n=32,h=16 at q~n^2 and q~n^3.
nodes.attack_surface: mechanical replay of the Modal window-selection rule over the configured h,n,q_exp grid
nodes.falsifier: a residual configured cell that is complete, under ceiling, and not already in the WSL-safe complete-cell list

nodes.title: M720 large h=3 gate replay
nodes.statement: The M720 gate arithmetic reproduces the two large h=3 banked calibration facts: full_census(128,3,17921)=18 anchored cores and full_census(256,3,65537)=129 anchored cores.
nodes.attack_surface: memory-controlled local exact replay of the two h=3 rows using compact sorted signature records
nodes.falsifier: a large h=3 replay returning a different anchored-core count, or a corrected banked calibration table

nodes.title: M720 local h=5 n=32 gate replay
nodes.statement: The M720 full_census arithmetic reproduces the locally bounded h=5 gate fact full_census(32,5,97)=96 unordered trades, all non-toral, with 30 anchored cores.
nodes.attack_surface: exact standalone replay of the C(32,5) row using the same elementary-signature definitions as modal_midlarge_h7_20.py
nodes.falsifier: a local exact replay of full_census(32,5,97) returning a count other than 96 unordered trades

nodes.title: M720 official h=7..20 paid/norm-gate dichotomy
nodes.statement: For official-shape h=7..20 active-core candidates, the x83 square-shift dichotomy applies: every candidate is either in the paid full-fiber/toral branch or is a primitive p-specific cleared-obstruction norm-gate event.
nodes.attack_surface: formal alignment of the M720 active-core equations with the universal x83 trade obstruction dichotomy; no row-prime GCD claim
nodes.falsifier: an official h=7..20 active-core candidate outside both the paid full-fiber branch and the x83 norm-gate branch

nodes.title: M720 MITM enumerator soundness
nodes.statement: For the parameter range used by modal_midlarge_h7_20.py (n<=1024, W<=n, and exponents encoded in 11-bit fields), mitm_slice is an exact anchored MITM enumerator over the window [0,W): it counts exactly disjoint anchored pairs P={0} union P', Q subset {1,...,W-1}, with equal e_1,...,e_{h-1} and unequal e_h, classifies toral pairs exactly as pairs of mu_h cosets, and marks complete only when W=n and the run did not abort.
nodes.attack_surface: static proof of the enumerator contract; independent of the expensive calibration count replay
nodes.falsifier: a window-contained anchored trade missed by mitm_slice, a spurious counted non-trade, or an incomplete slice marked complete

nodes.title: M720 complete bounded-band calibration certificates
nodes.statement: Complete calibration cells for h=7..20 have zero unpaid non-toral active cores. Any n=1024 window-slice result is explicitly labeled as slice-local and is not used as a global zero certificate.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the MITM gate, proved certificate semantics, and off-laptop complete zero certificates
nodes.falsifier: a complete calibration cell containing an unpaid non-toral active core, or a certificate that treats an incomplete window slice as a complete cell

nodes.title: M720 WSL-safe complete zero subcertificates
nodes.statement: The WSL-safe complete h=7..20 calibration cells selected by the Modal scan ceiling have complete=true and zero unpaid non-toral anchored active cores: n=16,h=7,8 at q~n^2 and q~n^3, and n=32,h=7 at q~n^2 and q~n^3.
nodes.attack_surface: local exact anchored MITM replay for the complete cells whose combination count is at most 6,000,000
nodes.falsifier: a WSL-safe complete calibration cell with nonzero unpaid non-toral anchored active cores, or a mismatched complete flag

nodes.title: M720 local n=16 small-gate replay
nodes.statement: The M720 full_census arithmetic reproduces the locally cheap n=16 gate facts: full_census(16,3,17)=352 trades, full_census(16,3,97)=16 trades, and full_census(16,4,17)=120 non-toral plus 6 toral.
nodes.attack_surface: exact standalone replay of the n=16 gate rows using the same elementary-signature definitions as modal_midlarge_h7_20.py
nodes.falsifier: a local exact replay of any n=16 gate row returning a different count

nodes.title: M720 h=3,4,5 ground-truth replay
nodes.statement: The M720 gate arithmetic reproduces the banked h=3,4,5 calibration facts: full_census(16,3,17)=352, full_census(16,3,97)=16, full_census(128,3,17921)=18 anchored cores, full_census(256,3,65537)=129 anchored cores, full_census(16,4,17)=120 non-toral + 6 toral, and full_census(32,5,97)=96 trades.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from local small-gate replay and the remaining off-laptop larger calibration rows
nodes.falsifier: any replay returning a different count, or a corrected banked calibration table

nodes.title: M720 remaining off-laptop gate replay
nodes.statement: The M720 gate arithmetic reproduces the remaining banked calibration facts by combining the local h=5 n=32 replay with the two large h=3 rows: full_census(128,3,17921)=18 anchored cores, full_census(256,3,65537)=129 anchored cores, and full_census(32,5,97)=96 trades.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the locally proved h=5 n=32 row and the still off-laptop h=3 large replay
nodes.falsifier: any remaining gate replay returning a different count, or a corrected banked calibration table

nodes.title: M720 residual off-laptop zero certificates
nodes.statement: Every h=7..20 calibration cell not covered by the WSL-safe complete zero subcertificates is either explicitly outside the complete-cell certificate set as a window slice, or is one of the over-ceiling n=32,h=16 complete-window cells with a separate zero certificate.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from residual slice metadata and over-ceiling complete-window certificates
nodes.falsifier: a residual complete calibration cell with nonzero unpaid non-toral active cores, or missing/false completeness metadata

nodes.title: E22 two-class exhaustion for worst-word challengers
nodes.statement: At the relevant low-slack crossing radii, every non-planted list word for the planted sunflower receiver lies in the structured E15 challenger class: mixed-petal or full-petal. Equivalently, after planted words are removed, there is no one-petal-only or background/core-only third challenger class. The proof uses root-count localization and the ratio-flat obstruction: a third-class word would need to replace r core roots by background roots with r < ell, but constancy on an ell-point petal would force a degree-r polynomial to vanish on ell distinct points, so the replacement is empty and the word is planted.
nodes.attack_surface: complete the E22 census remotely or prove directly from the interpolation/agreement equations that any non-planted crossing codeword touching at most one petal cannot reach agreement k+sigma
nodes.falsifier: an exhaustive cell with unclassified > 0, or an explicit low-slack word family outside planted plus mixed/full-petal classes

nodes.title: E22 dyadic local-to-common saturation
nodes.statement: In a dyadic E22 domain, if a fixed tail B is removed and each local non-tail block is saturated on full fibers of some dyadic quotient map x -> x^{M_i} with M_i>t, then the union of all local non-tail blocks is saturated on full fibers of the minimum local modulus M_* = min_i M_i, and M_*>t.
nodes.attack_surface: dyadic quotient moduli form a divisibility chain, so larger-modulus fibers refine into fibers of the minimum local modulus
nodes.falsifier: dyadic quotient moduli M_*<M_i where a full M_i-fiber is not a union of full M_*-fibers

nodes.title: E22 cross-scale pricing multiplicity
nodes.statement: The quotient staircase pricing column counts the canonical support-scale equivalence classes from e22_cross_scale_support_canonical_form with exactly the declared multiplicity, so quotienting by cross-scale duplicates neither overcounts nor undercounts E22 challengers. This is reduced to the proved dyadic minimal-scale selector plus the remaining pricing-column compatibility theorem.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from e22_dyadic_minimal_scale_selector and e22_minimal_scale_pricing_compatibility
nodes.falsifier: a canonical cross-scale equivalence class whose contribution to the pricing column has the wrong multiplicity

nodes.title: E22 nested-fiber residual identity
nodes.statement: For dyadic scales M_i<M_j, with every M_j-fiber a union of M_i-fibers, a support with canonical scale-M_i data is raw-admissible at M_j exactly when the scale-M_i tail plus the selected M_i-fibers whose M_j-parent is not completely selected has total size less than M_j.
nodes.attack_surface: finite nested-partition identity for canonical full-fiber recovery
nodes.falsifier: a coarse M_j-fiber fully contained in the support without all of its M_i-children selected, or a wrong residual-tail size formula

nodes.title: E22 quotient-fiber locator saturation
nodes.statement: In the multiplicative subgroup domain, for any divisor M of the domain size and any quotient value z in the image of x -> x^M, divisibility by the fiber locator X^M-z is equivalent to containing the entire fiber {x: x^M=z}. Products of distinct such factors are therefore exactly local unions of full quotient fibers.
nodes.attack_surface: elementary locator identity for quotient fibers in a cyclic subgroup
nodes.falsifier: a quotient value z whose fiber locator is not X^M-z, or a squarefree support locator divisible by X^M-z without containing the full fiber

nodes.title: E22 minimal-scale count formula
nodes.statement: The dyadic quotient staircase pricing column, with any declared duplicate subtraction or multiplicity factor, counts exactly the supports whose canonical tail data satisfy the minimal-scale tail criterion. This is reduced to the proved minimal-scale partition plus the remaining exact column-evaluation theorem.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the dyadic minimal-scale partition and the remaining exact pricing-column evaluation
nodes.falsifier: a tail-minimal support class omitted, duplicated, or assigned the wrong multiplicity by the refined pricing formula

nodes.title: E22 dyadic-chain minimal-scale accounting
nodes.statement: For a finite dyadic chain of admissible scales, raw per-scale counts decompose triangularly by minimal scale: the raw count at scale M_j equals the selected minimal count at M_j plus the contributions from smaller minimal scales that are also admissible at M_j. Therefore exact selected counts are recovered by subtracting the smaller-scale overlap counts.
nodes.attack_surface: formal partition accounting on a finite totally ordered set
nodes.falsifier: a class counted at scale M_j whose minimal admissible scale is larger than M_j, or a class with no unique minimal scale

nodes.title: E22 weighted intersection certificate soundness
nodes.statement: If a lower-scale intersection certificate gives, for every dyadic pair M_i<M_j and subset of smaller scales, a finite coefficient formula with a verified bijection to the residual-profile universe and the dyadic_profile_evaluation weight on every object, then e22_lower_scale_intersection_profile_counts holds.
nodes.attack_surface: finite weighted-sum and bijection soundness for lower-scale intersection certificates
nodes.falsifier: a verified bijective weighted intersection certificate whose coefficient formula does not equal the true weighted intersection count

nodes.title: E22 planted profile disjointness
nodes.statement: In the E22 planted sunflower receiver with distinct nonzero petal scalars, every planted codeword agrees on the core and exactly one full petal, with no background agreements and no second touched petal. Hence any nondegenerate mixed/full-petal staircase challenger is disjoint from the planted family.
nodes.attack_surface: direct profile computation for planted scalar multiples of the core locator
nodes.falsifier: a planted codeword with a mixed/full-petal nonplanted profile, a second petal agreement caused by repeated scalars, or a background agreement

nodes.title: E22 residual minimality/multiplicity filter
nodes.statement: Apply the lower-scale nonadmissibility conditions |B_M'(R)|>=M' for every dyadic M'<M_i and the declared dyadic_profile_evaluation multiplicity to the residual-profile generating function, yielding the exact overlap formula O_{i,j} for every dyadic pair M_i<M_j. This is reduced to the proved finite lower-scale inclusion-exclusion identity and the remaining exact intersection-profile counts with multiplicity.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from lower-scale inclusion-exclusion and the remaining intersection-profile formulas with pricing multiplicity
nodes.falsifier: a support passing the residual budget but failing minimality or multiplicity, or a minimal support removed by the filter

nodes.title: E22 tail-removed factor-manifest soundness
nodes.statement: If a manifest supplies one common tail B, dyadic local moduli M_i>t, verifies |B|<min_i M_i, and proves that each non-tail locator L_{T_i\B} equals a product of distinct quotient-fiber factors X^{M_i}-z, then e22_common_tail_invariance_payload holds.
nodes.attack_surface: formal conversion from explicit quotient-factor manifest to full-fiber and kernel-invariant non-tail sets
nodes.falsifier: a verified tail-removed quotient-factor manifest whose non-tail set is not kernel-invariant or whose tail/modulus data do not satisfy the common-tail payload

nodes.title: E22 dyadic minimal-scale selector
nodes.statement: For dyadic domains, the admissible quotient moduli form a finite chain, so every canonical support-scale class with at least one admissible modulus has a unique minimal admissible modulus. Selecting that modulus gives a canonical representative of the cross-scale class.
nodes.attack_surface: finite-chain selector on the canonical support-scale data
nodes.falsifier: a dyadic canonical support-scale class with two distinct minimal admissible moduli or no well-defined selected representative

nodes.title: E22 tail-coset staircase locator algebra
nodes.statement: If M|n, B is a tail of size |B|<M, and H is a set of quotient fibers of x -> x^M in the multiplicative subgroup domain, then the locator of B together with the full fibers over H is L_B(X)G(X^M). If M>t, its top t subleading coefficients depend only on B and the number of selected fibers, not on which fibers are selected.
nodes.attack_surface: finite locator algebra and top-coefficient gap M>t
nodes.falsifier: a tail-plus-full-coset support whose locator is not L_B(X)G(X^M), or whose top t coefficients depend on the selected quotient roots despite M>t

nodes.title: E22 fixed-scale staircase injectivity
nodes.statement: For a fixed quotient modulus M, the locator L_B(X)G(X^M) of a tail |B|<M plus full quotient fibers determines the selected fibers and the tail uniquely. Equivalently, there are no hidden multiplicities inside one fixed M staircase scale.
nodes.attack_surface: unique factorization/root-set recovery at fixed M
nodes.falsifier: two distinct tails or selected quotient-fiber sets at the same M with the same locator

nodes.title: E22 minimal-scale overlap count formulas
nodes.statement: For each dyadic pair of scales M_i<M_j, compute exactly the number of support classes whose minimal admissible scale is M_i and which are also counted by the raw quotient staircase summand at M_j. This is reduced to the proved nested-fiber residual identity and the remaining exact residual-profile formula, including all declared duplicate or multiplicity factors used by dyadic_profile_evaluation.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the nested-fiber residual identity and the remaining exact residual-profile formulas
nodes.falsifier: a smaller-minimal-scale class contributing to a larger raw scale with an unaccounted or wrongly weighted overlap

nodes.title: E22 kernel-invariance full-fiber criterion
nodes.statement: In a cyclic E22 domain, for any dyadic divisor M, a subset is a union of full fibers of x -> x^M if and only if it is invariant under multiplication by the M-th-root kernel of that map.
nodes.attack_surface: finite cyclic-group quotient criterion for unions of cosets
nodes.falsifier: a subset invariant under the quotient-map kernel that is not a union of fibers, or a union of fibers that is not kernel-invariant

nodes.title: E22 lower-scale intersection formula payload
nodes.statement: For every dyadic pair M_i<M_j and every subset of smaller dyadic scales, the dyadic-tree tail polynomial gives exact weighted coefficient formulas for the residual profiles raw-admissible at M_j and admissible at the selected lower scales, with atom weights supplied by dyadic_profile_evaluation.
nodes.attack_surface: verify the structural intersection formulas for all subsets of smaller dyadic scales, with dyadic_profile_evaluation multiplicities
nodes.falsifier: a missing subset, an incorrect coefficient formula, or a multiplicity mismatch with dyadic_profile_evaluation

nodes.title: E22 lower-scale filter inclusion-exclusion
nodes.statement: Inside any fixed residual-profile universe for a dyadic pair M_i<M_j, the condition that no smaller dyadic scale M'<M_i is admissible is exactly the finite inclusion-exclusion complement of the smaller-scale admissibility events. The identity is valid with the declared multiplicity weights as long as the intersection counts use those same weights.
nodes.attack_surface: finite set inclusion-exclusion for the complement of smaller-scale admissibility events
nodes.falsifier: a weighted residual universe where inclusion-exclusion fails to remove precisely the objects admissible at some smaller scale

nodes.title: E22 minimal-scale pricing compatibility
nodes.statement: The dyadic quotient staircase pricing column, as evaluated by dyadic_profile_evaluation, counts the minimal-scale representatives selected by e22_dyadic_minimal_scale_selector with exactly the declared multiplicity. This is reduced to the proved tail-minimality criterion plus the remaining count formula for supports satisfying that criterion.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from e22_minimal_scale_tail_criterion and e22_minimal_scale_count_formula
nodes.falsifier: a selected canonical representative that is omitted, duplicated, or assigned the wrong multiplicity by the dyadic pricing column

nodes.title: E22 minimal-scale tail criterion
nodes.statement: For dyadic E22 support-scale data, a modulus M is the selected minimal admissible scale exactly when |B_M(R)|<M and every smaller admissible dyadic modulus M'<M has |B_{M'}(R)|>=M'.
nodes.attack_surface: finite-chain reformulation of the canonical full-fiber/tail recovery rule
nodes.falsifier: a support whose selected minimal scale disagrees with the tail-size criterion

nodes.title: E22 cross-scale support canonical form
nodes.statement: For any E22 staircase support R, the set of quotient moduli M>t for which R has a tail-plus-full-M-fibers representation is recovered canonically from R by taking all M with tail B_M=R minus the union of full M-fibers contained in R satisfying |B_M|<M. Equal-support cross-scale representations therefore have the same canonical support-scale data.
nodes.attack_surface: fixed-scale full-fiber recovery applied simultaneously over all quotient moduli
nodes.falsifier: a support R with a staircase representation at M that is not recovered by the full-fiber/tail rule, or equal supports with different canonical scale data

nodes.title: E22 residual-profile generating function
nodes.statement: For fixed dyadic M_i<M_j, before imposing lower-scale minimality, canonical scale-M_i data raw-admissible at M_j are counted exactly by the product generating function that chooses selected M_i-fibers by complete or incomplete M_j-parent and then chooses canonical tails in unselected M_i-fibers subject to b+M_i*r<M_j.
nodes.attack_surface: finite product enumeration over coarse parents and unselected fine fibers
nodes.falsifier: a canonical scale-M_i support counted by the product formula with the wrong residual statistic or tail multiplicity

nodes.title: E22 agreement cofactor equations
nodes.statement: For any listed E22 sunflower codeword f and its zero-agreement root set Z inside core union background, writing f=U L_Z converts every petal agreement on petal i into the cofactor equation U(x)L_{Z\C}(x)=a_i L_{C\Z}(x) after cancelling the common core factor. Thus the mixed/full-petal support-forcing problem may be studied entirely through these cofactor equations on petal agreement points.
nodes.attack_surface: direct locator factorization and cancellation in the local sunflower model
nodes.falsifier: a listed codeword and petal agreement point for which the stated cofactor equation fails after factoring zero agreements

nodes.title: E22 common-tail invariance certificate soundness
nodes.statement: If an E22 cofactor-tail certificate specifies a common tail B, dyadic local moduli M_i>t, verifies |B|<min_i M_i, and verifies that each T_i\B is invariant under the M_i-th-root kernel, then e22_cofactor_common_tail_kernel_invariance holds.
nodes.attack_surface: finite certificate semantics for common-tail, tail-bound, local-modulus, and kernel-invariance data
nodes.falsifier: a verified common-tail invariance certificate that does not imply the E22 common-tail kernel-invariance predicate

nodes.title: E22 minimal-scale column evaluation
nodes.statement: The dyadic quotient staircase pricing column, as evaluated by dyadic_profile_evaluation and with the declared duplicate subtraction or multiplicity factor, equals the sum over the disjoint minimal-scale partition cells from e22_minimal_scale_partition. This is reduced to the proved dyadic-chain accounting identity plus the remaining exact cross-scale overlap count formulas.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from dyadic-chain triangular accounting and the remaining exact overlap-count inputs
nodes.falsifier: a minimal-scale partition cell whose contribution to the dyadic pricing column is omitted, duplicated, or assigned the wrong multiplicity

nodes.title: E22 cofactor petal divisibility
nodes.statement: For each touched petal in an E22 mixed/full-petal challenger, the cofactor equation U(x)L_{Z\C}(x)=a_i L_{C\Z}(x) on the agreement subset T_i is equivalent to divisibility of H_i(X)=U(X)L_{Z\C}(X)-a_i L_{C\Z}(X) by the locator L_{T_i}(X). In particular, a full touched petal contributes the full petal locator as a divisor of H_i.
nodes.attack_surface: formal polynomial root/divisibility translation from pointwise cofactor equations
nodes.falsifier: a touched-petal agreement set where H_i vanishes on every agreement point but is not divisible by the agreement-set locator

nodes.title: E22 minimal-scale class partition
nodes.statement: For dyadic E22 support-scale data, the classes with at least one admissible quotient modulus decompose disjointly by their unique minimal admissible modulus. Equivalently, the tail-minimal predicates from e22_minimal_scale_tail_criterion form a disjoint partition of the selected canonical representatives.
nodes.attack_surface: finite-chain partition induced by the dyadic minimal-scale selector
nodes.falsifier: a dyadic support class with two different minimal admissible moduli, or with an admissible modulus but no tail-minimal partition cell

nodes.title: E22 lower-scale intersection profile counts
nodes.statement: For every dyadic pair M_i<M_j and every subset of smaller dyadic scales M'<M_i, compute the exact weighted count of residual-profile data that are raw-admissible at M_j and simultaneously admissible at all scales in that subset, using the dyadic_profile_evaluation multiplicity convention. This is reduced to weighted-intersection certificate soundness plus the remaining formula payload.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the proved weighted-intersection certificate rule and the remaining exact formula payload
nodes.falsifier: an intersection of lower-scale admissibility events counted with the wrong size or multiplicity, or a defect in the certificate soundness rule

nodes.title: E22 cross-scale root-set recovery
nodes.statement: If two E22 staircase locators at possibly different quotient moduli are equal, then they have the same root set; applying fixed-scale recovery at each modulus recovers the corresponding tail and selected fibers at each scale. Thus any hidden multiplicity is exactly a cross-scale equality of recovered root-set data.
nodes.attack_surface: locator root-set uniqueness plus fixed-scale recovery
nodes.falsifier: equal staircase locators with different root sets, or a fixed-scale tail/fiber recovery failure

nodes.title: E22 tail-removed quotient-factor passthrough
nodes.statement: If a touched-petal locator L_T divides a cofactor H, and T is the disjoint union of a tail B and full quotient fibers with locators X^{M}-z, then after removing the tail factor L_B the cofactor divisibility supplies exactly those quotient factors. Products of distinct quotient fibers pass through with their multiplicity one.
nodes.attack_surface: formal squarefree locator divisibility after splitting tail and quotient-fiber factors
nodes.falsifier: a squarefree locator factorization L_T=L_B prod_z (X^M-z) dividing H while one quotient factor fails to divide the tail-removed cofactor

nodes.title: E22 overlap residual-profile formula
nodes.statement: For every dyadic pair M_i<M_j, count exactly the canonical minimal-scale-M_i E22 support data whose scale-M_i tail and incomplete-M_j-parent residual profile satisfy the raw M_j admissibility inequality from e22_overlap_nested_fiber_residual_identity, with the declared dyadic_profile_evaluation multiplicity. This is reduced to the proved residual generating function and the remaining lower-scale minimality/multiplicity filter.
nodes.attack_surface: assembly from the residual generating function and the remaining minimality/multiplicity filter
nodes.falsifier: a minimal-scale-M_i support whose residual profile is counted with the wrong binomial coefficient or multiplicity

nodes.title: Subfield/trace-flat paid gate (SHARED: dli + sov)
nodes.statement: Over extension rows F_q=F_{p^k} (k>=2), the TRACE-FLAT/subfield-degenerate locus is a PAID class OR reduces to the base F_p by Weil restriction: (frequency form, dli) lambda with Tr_{F_q/F_p}(lambda*phi(x)) == 0 on a positive-density section-image; (cell form, sov) cells where x->Tr(ax) is constant on a positive-density free-root set. VERIFIED these are non-negligible: F_{p^2} trace-zero lambda gives S=Omega(L_j) (P2); F_{17^32} canonical trace char is flat on 496/512 grid points, cell density 0.506 at h=21 (P3). Prime rows (k=1): VACUOUS (Tr=id, no proper subfield). SUPPORTED by the proved interleaved/Weil-restriction machinery that closed f1 (base reduction).
nodes.attack_surface: Weil-restrict extension rows to base F_p (proved f1/interleaved machinery); or price the trace-flat/subfield-norm class in the paid ledger.
nodes.falsifier: An extension row whose trace-flat/subfield-degenerate locus is neither paid nor base-reducible.
nodes.notes: PROVED (Pro T1, all core claims verified): exact Weil descent + trace-flat classification |Ann(A)|=p^{e-dimW} + SOV Euler product; both counterexamples absorbed as paid trace-kernel collapses. TRACE_FLAT ledger column installed; affordability (2^122.5 << |F|=2^131) routed to the mca_safe budget audit. SHARED SPINE CLOSED — dli & sov now need only their residual.

nodes.title: Dihedral fixed-point sibling window certificate (P6)
nodes.statement: For every sigma in [2^33, sigma*], a dihedral/Chebyshev quotient-core family (twin fibers x+x^{-1}, plus one inversion fixed point at the endpoint) whose members are valid degree<k=2^40 RS codewords and which gives > floor(q_line/2^128) bad witnesses at agreement k+sigma, uniformly for log2 q in (255.900, 256). Obligations: (1) Chebyshev degree audit deg<k; (2) distinctness/noncontainment vs the old 2-power qcore witnesses; (3) strict-window endpoint sigma<d with d=sigma+1 and the fixed-point sibling supplying the top-endpoint parity d=sigma*+1; (4) priced count log2 C(2^40,2^32+1)~4.05e10 >> 128 (VERIFIED). NON-AQB (single-family witnesses, no averaging), non-giant-M.
nodes.attack_surface: Chebyshev locator degree bookkeeping; inversion fixed-point endpoint parity; distinctness audit vs quotient-core; binomial count certificate.
nodes.falsifier: A sigma in [2^33, sigma*] where every dihedral twin/fixed-point family either overflows degree k, collapses to the old qcore count, or fails noncontainment.
nodes.notes: P6 residual leaf: the concrete certificate that turns the dihedral sibling window into a theorem. Entropy margin overwhelming (verified); the work is degree/distinctness certification, not numerical. | REFUTED (Pro T8, verified): packet size != Chebyshev drop (twin-pair packet drop=d/2, overflow 2^32 at first band sigma); true Dickson fibers <=2gcd(r,n) collapse to old 2-power qcore; next scale M=2^34 unpriced (C(127,64)=2^123.17<2^128). NO positive certificate.

nodes.title: Quotient-cofactor normal form (SHARED TERMINAL: e22 + petal)
nodes.statement: For D=mu_n, M|n, char∤n: f(eta x)=eta^e f(x) for all eta in mu_M IFF f=X^e G(X^M) (isotypic projector Pi_{M,e}), and off a bounded tail the retained root set is a union of full x->x^M fibers with locator prod_z(X^M - z). This is the shared terminal consumed by BOTH the e22 factor manifest and the petal paid-quotient ledger. NOTE: the NAIVE sharing (dimension/quotient-compatible-constraints alone => scalar pullback) is REFUTED (verified counterexample P(X^M)(A(X^M)+X^r B(X^M)): full-fiber divisible, support in 2 classes). Character selection needs SOURCE data — so e22 (pointwise covariance E22-COV) and petal (family chargeability P5-CL) stay SEPARATE upstream leaves.
nodes.attack_surface: proved via mu_M isotypic projectors + full-fiber factorization (char∤n).
nodes.falsifier: a mu_M-character-component polynomial that is not X^e G(X^M), or a covariant retained root set that is not a fiber union.
nodes.notes: PROVED (Pro T2, verified F_37). Shared TERMINAL of e22+petal; the sources do NOT merge (naive sharing refuted). Mild generalization of kernel_invariance_full_fiber_criterion to twist e!=0. | 2nd T2 pass CONFIRMS the split (T2a=this, T2b=e22_mixed_petal_covariance, T2c=petal_cofactor_chargeability). 2nd counterexample (affine family A_c={1+X+sum a_k X^k}: dim c-1 grows but support in classes 0,1 mod M => never a pullback, VERIFIED) reinforces that dimension-excess alone forces nothing on the P5 side.

nodes.title: CONJECTURE B-WEAK: the endpoint budget for the U-weighted joint skew mass at the official rows (the corridor floor)
nodes.statement: RE-POSED AS THE FLOOR CONJECTURE (2026-07-07, user decision; derivation + pre-registered falsifier in REPOSE_B_WEAK.md): at the official prize rows, q^{-t+H} * W_cen <= 2^122 — the U-weighted central-profile mass of TRUE valid-skew counts taken JOINTLY across the tower (E_U[prod_j rho_j] inside the endpoint budget), which is EXACTLY and ONLY what the x4_exactlist_staircase_split interface consumes from this frontier (1 of its 19 wired inputs; the packet records that nothing sharper is needed — 'polynomial-loss per level suffices'). NO per-level bound, factorization, uniform constant, or route structure is asserted: all such strengthenings are strictly stronger statements whose fates do not bear on this conjecture (nine adversarial rounds killed only strengthenings; B itself never scratched — strongest priced attack: 2.25 bits of 122; every exactly-computed extreme row <= 0.015 bits). The conjecture stands until refuted per the PRE-REGISTERED standard: a sustained super-polylog per-junction growth trend in the JOINT loss of the packet's own nested toy towers (>= 3 q-scales, >= 2 depths, coset column routed through the exact accounting), or a replayable construction whose transported arithmetic exceeds 2^122 at the official rows. Route material (C1', C2'', the S1 norm-sieve theorem, moment transfer, the kill-lemmas) lives on the evidence lane and may be replaced freely; promotion of any route to a conditional close requires surviving its own adversarial rounds (F-round rule). Full nine-round history: DLI_CLOSE_PINNED.md; falsification dossier: notes/F_ROUND_CONJECTURE_FALSIFICATION.md. | SURVIVAL +1 (2026-07-07, notes/BWEAK_EXPERIMENT_1.md): the first direct joint attack (pre-registered scaling study, ~35 exact tower jobs, 3 depths x up to 5 q-scales) found NO refutation signal — the naive 43.9x trend at t=2 classified down to Poisson-consistent primitive accidents carrying deterministic quotient-staircase coset dressing (the packet's own charged column, observed in the wild: one size-10 accident orbit dressed 2^3 x 32 = 256 ways, histogram exact); deep sub-volume bands empty at t=3,4. The experiment independently validates the x4 four-column design from the joint side. | SURVIVAL +2 (2026-07-07, F1-exp2): engineered stacked towers (10 rows, exact D3 both levels) — lift structure r-diluted exactly (E64 = 1.000000 everywhere); measured level-32 excess within the lift-aware ledger + the q-independent forced-window constant (0.598 = sum C(16,w>=8)/2^16) at 9/10 rows, worst 1.32x; the 2.8x candidate signal was the omitted mean-field constant (classification catch #4). | SURVIVAL +3 -> **HARDENED** (2026-07-07, F1-A3): dual-spectrum population sweep (24 rows, full exact dyadic spectra, 5 bands) — median K(j) in [0.6, 1.35] everywhere; no structural surplus. Families: joint scaling study / engineered stacked towers / dual-spectrum sweep. | SURVIVAL +4 (2026-07-07, Pro floor-window 1): independent Pro attack (own exact concentration instrument, cross-scale + stress rows) — no kill; the single K=16 spike at q=15249281 replayed exactly and classified as an orbit-quantized Poisson accident (P = 0.0072/row) by both sides independently. | CATCH #13 — EVALUATION-FIELD BETA-CLAUSE (2026-07-07, correspondence beta-round, nodes/dli_prime_weighted_large_block_support/notes/f1_beta_check.md): the consumer's flatness clause 'L_j genuinely independent F_q-conditions' is FALSE at base-domain official extension rows — Q_d in F_p[x] (domain-combinatorial objects) and zeta^(2l-1) in F_p(zeta), [F_p(zeta):F_p] <= 2 (= F_p exactly at the KoalaBear-shaped row), so each condition is an F_p(zeta)-condition at density 1/|F_p(zeta)|, mis-priced by (q/p)^(L_j) = 2^(154.9 L_j) there. FIX: the budget normalizer and the flatness model read the evaluation field as the generated field F_p(D, zeta); at generating rows (ALL nine adversarial rounds + every toy tower) the readings coincide — the evidence ledger is untouched; non-generating rows route through the f1/ext descent. Graded honestly: model-clause exposure proved by field confinement; no explicit super-budget B_j(M) constructed (the b2b port to F_p^2 was deliberately not launched); the sibling F7 census verifies the identical mechanism.
nodes.attack_surface: Attack B directly, not its strengthenings: (1) the junction-correlation scaling study on the JOINT object (exact toy towers, q-sweep x depth-sweep vs (C log q)^J with exact coset routing) — the F-round's growing conditional ratios are the live threat to chase; (2) engineered stacked towers (multi-level norm coincidences) priced against the transported budget; (3) any structural mechanism outside the killed families (norm-engineering, boundary-volume, clusters, dual-geometric are all priced/killed).
nodes.falsifier: Pre-registered (REPOSE_B_WEAK.md): sustained super-polylog per-junction growth of E_U[prod rho] in the packet's nested toy towers across >= 3 q-scales and >= 2 depths with exact coset accounting; or any replayable construction transporting past 2^122 at the official rows. Route/proxy failures explicitly do NOT count.
nodes.notes: The deepest DLI leaf (~7th reduction). Concrete + possibly computationally checkable. Consistent with calibration eta*/L=3.6e-7. | EMPIRICAL EVIDENCE (local census, seconds-scale, this session): (1) Deligne per-block cancellation CONFIRMED — max_lambda|mu_hat_S| ~ 2.5/sqrt(|S|) for the odd-eval phase, ROBUST across random AND structured (consecutive-index) subsets (no adversarial block cancels worse; section values are mult-structured => additively spread). (2) Non-cancelling blocks are only the SMALL ones (|S| <= ~C^2 ~ 9). (3) Near-peak FREQUENCY count VANISHES with scale: n=32 has ~212 freqs with |mu|>0.3, n=128 has 0, n=256+ none with |mu|>0.2 (max|mu| ~2.5/sqrt(n)). So the near-peak mass DECREASES with scale (the ~129 is a small-scale artifact). Strong support for the dli equidistribution core; the exact multi-level block partition not directly tested (needs the D-thread partition def). | REAL-OBJECT CORRECTION (harness-based, this session): my earlier max|mu_hat|~2.5/sqrt(n) evidence was a PROXY (whole-section average), NOT the real DLI object. The real object is B_j(M)=q^{-L_j} sum_lambda prod_y mu_hat_y(lambda), mu_hat_y=sum_d psi(d lambda v_y) (VERIFIED via verify_level2_tower.py TEST 1 = exact census match). Excess rho=skewcount*q/2^|G| SCALES WITH q (not 1/sqrt(n)); eta_j=log_q rho_j. Governed by VANDERMONDE RIGIDITY: nonzero skew needs support >= L_j+1, so the meaningful regime is LARGE support (my |G|=3-6 test was below-threshold toy artifacts). Calibration eta*/L=3.6e-7 => eta_j=3.6e-7 L_j (linear, tiny coeff) => sum=o(t) via the small coefficient, NOT bounded excess. CONFIDENCE: HIGH->MEDIUM (proxy was misleading). NEXT: reproduce the calibration eta_j=3.6e-7 L_j on the real object at the actual calibration params. | REAL-OBJECT CONFIRMATION (Fourier form, verify_d3_reduction harness extended): the deviation exponent eta*=log_q rho_j is FLAT — rho_j -> 1.0000 (eta*->0) as N/scale grows, for BOTH ternary and signed domains. CALIBRATION REPRODUCED EXACTLY: signed midpoint eta*=0.019 at mu_32 N=16 (exact match to the node record), DECREASING with N; ternary flat ~0. So Sum_j eta_j = o(t) holds on the REAL object (prod_y mu_hat_y(lambda), not the earlier whole-section proxy). CONFIDENCE: MEDIUM->HIGH (now on the true object, calibration on the nose). The leaf is CORRECT as stated — no chain-hacking needed. | PRO CONDITIONAL CLOSURE (verified): exact reduction rho_j=q^L|Z_j|/U proved (Lemma 1); dli-flatness reduced to the RES resultant-survivor count; zero-atom caveat => rank-charged central measure; fixed-lambda refuted. The remaining leaf is now a sharp COUNTING statement, empirically supported. | ZERO-ATOM RESOLVED (verified): Pro's singleton-{0} counterexample was a phrasing artifact of my brief ("bounded symmetric domain" did not say ACTIVE). Real central profiles have active coords |D_y|>=2 and the wide Vandermonde odd-eval matrix A is FULL RANK (rank=L for all N>=L, verified) => rank-charging penalty L_j-rank=0. No real gap; scope = active full-rank central profiles (auto-satisfied). The RES count is unaffected. | MAJOR CORRECTION (verified): sup formulation REFUTED by a low-mass profile (rho_j=(q/3)^L, mass-forced). My HIGH confidence was overstated (only tested full-mass). The sufficient-condition route to b2b is dead; the real obligation is the U-weighted average = b2b directly, OPEN. Lemma 1 exact reduction still holds. This is the leaf-is-incorrect -> reassess scenario. | CODEX CROSS-VALIDATION (independent): reproduced the zero-atom/rank-deficient falsifier (matches Pro + my sup refutation); active full-rank large-support scans show the small-support spikes FLATTEN (active 18->36: rho 129->1.04; signed L=4 active 32->40: rho 4.06->1.15). Positive evidence that the rank-charged/weighted-average (large-support) obligation holds -- no large-support falsifier found. | DLI-CLOSE PINNED 2026-07-06 (see DLI_CLOSE_PINNED.md): the closing target is W_j <= 3 (2^-weight count of odd-trades on the no-antipode section), with three verified-equivalent displays (trades / q^L Pr[Ad=0] closed form / sum-of-cos^2 analytic). Weight-split architecture: w<=L PROVED empty; gap L<w<=~68L claimed empty (structure-hunted clean at 5 random-empty rows; window law calibrated -- accidents match random exactly, u2c pattern); tail needs count <= K x random OR the analytic route with the 1-bit/coordinate margin (circle constant -2 vs -1 needed). All known falsifier families pre-empted by verified section hypotheses (no antipodes, no cosets) + prime scoping. Level-independence flagged as explicit hypothesis. | DLI-CLOSE ROUND 2 (Pro response verified 2026-07-06): W<=3 refuted as stated (verified witness: 110 relation-trades at q=65537 + the identity bound) -- a normalization artifact; RE-POSED to the row-uniform E-form: E_U[rho_j] <= 4 per level (= sum_{lambda!=0} T <= 3), which SURVIVES the counterexample row with E=1.000000 exactly and needs no top-prime condition. Pro uniform DLI-AC also falsified at toys (near-peak concentration, -6.9 bits); the round-3 kernel = near-peak mass control in SUM form. Tennis: sup->weighted, W->E, uniform-lambda->summed-lambda -- uniform dies, average survives, thrice. | CODEX GAP-ATTACK integrated (paused run, all 6 attacks, ZERO falsifiers): 10 more window-closed rows clean (15 total); ROTATION FAMILY ALGEBRAICALLY KILLED (odd r0 invertible mod 2^s => rotation trivial -- a proof); tail constant K ~ 1 measured (max 4.3 small-row, ~1.0 at n=64 with counts to 73k); uniform-AC falsification independently confirmed by exhaustive worst-lambda census (912k lambdas); level-independence honestly still-open; D2=D3 exact at 3 new rows. See notes/dli_gap_attack_20260706/. | ROUND 4 (Pro DLI-NPM refutation verified + diagnosed): the witness (N=12 sub-segment) violates the balanced-volume condition 2^N >= q^L -- random 12-subsets fail too (alpha-ratio, not structure); the FULL half-section at the same row survives (E=1.348). Target re-scoped to the production row class R* (rotated full half-sections, 2^N >= q^L -- the real dyadic tower levels are exactly these). Fifth window-law instance.
