nodes.title: FLOOR CONJECTURE (EXTRAS-BUDGET): non-coset-union t-null extras <= n^3 = 2^123 at official prize-max rows
nodes.statement: FLOOR RE-POSE (2026-07-07, floor campaign — notes/floor_campaign/SURVEY_X4_CLUSTER.md): the consumer (x4 via b2_modp_giant_extras) consumes ONLY the count '#{non-coset-union t-null blocks + trade families} <= n^3 = 2^123 at official prize-max rows'. The repaired dichotomy (ZERO accidents at sub-balance rows) is a STRICTLY STRONGER route statement — kept below as route material. This floor stands until refuted per the PRE-REGISTERED standard: sub-balance scaled rows with extras exceeding the transported n^3-scaled budget, sustained across >= 3 scales; above-balance window accidents (the known 2026-07-06 witness family) do NOT count. Opening evidence: prize rows sub-balance by ~2% (accident window EMPTY at every block size); boundary scan extras 192 -> 64 -> 0 past the window; complementation + rigidity lemmas PROVED. | RE-POSED 2026-07-06 (FALSIFIED AS STATED, verified witness -- see FALSIFICATION_AND_REPOSE.md): the unscoped dichotomy is FALSE: at (q=97,n=32,t=2) there are GIANT (81%) t-null non-coset-union blocks (complements of size-6 accidents; 160 witnesses). Accidents are random-model objects of the ABOVE-BALANCE window C(n,b) > q^t. REPAIRED CLAIM: at rows with q^t >= 2^n (sub-balance everywhere; all official prize-max rows qualify by ~2%): every t-null block is a union of mu_M-cosets (M >= t) with zero-sum value patterns at multiples of M <= t. PROVED ASSETS: complementation lemma (S t-null iff C\S t-null; sparse product + Newton; verified 27762/27762), top-band rigidity (|S| >= n-t forces the full coset), (t+1)-support rigidity (t-null of size t+1 = full mu_(t+1)-coset, empty when t+1 does not divide n). Census: t=3 and large-q/n rows CLEAN. Remaining obligation: entropic suppression -- zero accidents in the sub-balance regime (anti-concentration, same shape as the dli RES count). | SURVIVAL +1 (2026-07-07, F2-A1, notes/f2a1_subbalance_sweep_modal.py): calibrated complete MITM window sweep — calibration gate reproduces the banked 192/64/0 transition EXACTLY; extras identically ZERO through the entire sub-balance regime (W from 1 down to 2^-14.5) at four cells. The floor's sub-balance claim holds with margin at every measured point. | SURVIVAL +2 (2026-07-07, F2-A2): engineered accident-selection (the dli round-5 norm-gate transported; 1440 trials, positively controlled against the known p=257 window accident) finds ZERO candidate sub-balance primes — the multi-condition ideals are generically coprime; the engineering channel is structurally empty, mirroring the E2 finding. | SURVIVAL +3 -> **HARDENED** (2026-07-07, F2-A3b): complete widest-window (b=16, 6e8 subsets) censuses — zero extras at every globally-sub-balance prime with the structural count pinned at 700 exactly (q-independent); window population confirmed below balance. Three distinct attack families absorbed; the EXTRAS-BUDGET floor is the campaign's first HARDENED conjecture. | SURVIVAL +5 (2026-07-07, Pro window-2 follow-up): the giant-block surface CLOSED BY PROOF — exact complement involution preserves t-nullity and coset-union status (E_b = E_{N-b}); verified fully incl. three t=5 Pocklington rows and an exhaustive N=16 check. Route material: the involution lemma feeds the structural-700 derivation. | CATCH #11 — WINDOW BETA-NORMALIZATION (2026-07-07, M_B audit, notes/correspondence/MB_VS_F1_LEDGER.md): the sub-balance window must be read at the GENERATED field B0 = F_p(D), i.e. |B0|^t >= 2^n, NOT at the ambient q. At base-domain official extension rows the two diverge: at the KoalaBear-shaped row (n=2^21, q=p^6, D in a coset of F_p*) the q-window admits the row (t*log2q = 2,097,314 >= n at t=11280) while the true t-null value space is p^t (PROVED: power sums confined to h^j*F_p by scaling conjugacy) — above balance by 2^1,740,627; pigeonhole forces a depth-t prefix fiber of >= 2^1,740,616 blocks vs coset unions <= 2^182 and budget n^3 = 2^63. The frozen q-scale wording is therefore untenable at such rows; the floor stands at generated-field-window rows (all hardening evidence was at prime rows, B0 = F). Consumer rule: x4/b2_modp_giant_extras consumes F2 only where |B0|^t >= 2^n; base-domain extension rows route through the f1/ext descent (s6 imported window). This is upstream's own beta-correction (V13 prop:capg-census-floor) transported to our ledger by the seam audit. || ENERGY-DICHOTOMY ADDENDUM (2026-07-09): the entropic-suppression obligation SPLITS via the effective BSG/quasicube instrument (f2_effective_energy_dichotomy, PROVED composition, printed constants): (a) HIGH-ENERGY branch CLOSED — any super-budget (> n^3 = 2^123 prize-max) accident family is forced 2^7.75-far (weakest instantiation) from maximal additive energy; few-structured-pieces shapes (<= 2^3.8 pieces) dead outright; (b) LOW-ENERGY residual: large additively-dissociated t-null families in sub-balance — the Fourier-flatness/major-arc shape, where the ~2% sub-balance margin must work. The kernel obligation is now branch (b) only. || F2-CAMPAIGN L1 (2026-07-09, f2_l1_fourier_spectrum_modal.py, F2_L1_FOURIER_SPECTRUM_PASS, 14 cells, falsifier NOT fired): the Fourier spectrum of the t=2 moment map at the calibration family — pre-registered kill criterion (generic max r > 32 at 3 scales, b=3) missed by 4-8x (3.8/6.6/8.4); the top generic arcs sit at HIGH-kappa chords (collision law #distinct = n - kappa(-l1/l2) verified exactly); kappa_max tracks n^2/q (10.6->4, 5.3->3, 4->4) — a Jacobi-sum-shaped chord bound is the L2 target; exact inversion gate reproduced the banked b=6 accident family (160 witnesses) and zero extras at every sub-balance cell. L3 DESIGN DATUM: absolute-mass inversion is lossy (N0=0 cells carry ~20 units of |E| mass) — L3 must route via Parseval/second moment, i.e. the SP-shaped census power identity. || F2-CAMPAIGN L2b STRATA (2026-07-09, f2_l2b_kappa_strata_modal.py, F2_L2B_KAPPA_STRATA_PASS, 7 cells): TWO CATCHES against the L1 reading, banked per protocol — (i) top-arc vs kappa is NOT monotone (97: kappa=4 stratum beats kappa=5,6; 193/b=6: kappa=3 beats 4); (ii) Chebyshev membership does NOT discriminate at t=2 (argmax chords mostly non-Cheb) — L2c re-posed. DISCOVERY: kappa and per-chord arc maxima are mu_n-ORBIT functions — the generic chord space collapses to (q-1)/n orbits (3/6/8 orbits at q=97/193/257, strata partition exact; max_r = mean_r within every single-orbit stratum); the quotient chord (c=0, kappa=n/2) dominates every cell and grows with b (6.7->14.4 at 97; 12.5->20.5 at 257) while generic strata stay bounded. L2a RE-POSED: Jacobi-sum evaluation of kappa PER ORBIT + per-orbit arc bound; the algebraic (quotient) arcs enter L3's Parseval budget exactly (they are only q-1 arcs). || F2-CAMPAIGN L2a+L2b' (2026-07-09): TWO PROVED RUNGS BANKED — the chord-orbit lemma (f2_chord_orbit_lemma: exact Jacobi formula for kappa per mu_n-orbit, error term with EXACT modulus sqrt(q); the generic chord geometry is an m-point computation) and the Weil-Newton arc bound (f2_weil_newton_arc_bound: |E_b| <= prod(2 sqrt q + r)/b! UNIFORM in lambda != 0 via the dilation identity + Newton majorization). CONSEQUENCE: the extreme block-size bands b <= 5 and b >= n-5 are PAID EXACTLY at official rows (~2^107 total vs the 2^123 budget). Branch (b) residual now = the MID-BAND census only, to be attacked via Parseval/SP (the census power identity) + the banked energy dichotomy. Ladder state: L1 done, L2a done, L2b' done (supersedes the per-orbit plan with a uniform bound), L2c dropped (Chebyshev non-discriminating), L3 mid-band open, L4 constants open, L5 assembly open. || F2-CAMPAIGN L3/M1 CALIBRATION (2026-07-09, f2_l3_m1_moment_calibration_modal.py, F2_L3_M1_CALIBRATION_PASS, 10 cells, exact full-census DP): M1 (moment ladder) VIABLE per the pre-registered read — census L2-flat at every cell (M2/flat 0.91-1.17); sub-balance maxima Poisson-consistent (no heavy nonzero fibers anywhere); the 0-fiber is EXACTLY the structured count at every cell (coset-union 8s at b=4; the banked 160/64 accident families reproduced at above-balance contrast cells). Confirms the L3 design: the mid-band obligation is confined to the specialness of s=0; generic-fiber flatness is the L2/SP statement, the 0-fiber's structured mass is exact combinatorics + the proved giant-block closure. PROVED NO-GO also banked (F2_L3_DESIGN.md): raw Parseval gives only sqrt(C(n,b)) — any L3 proof must use the value-0 specialness, never generic fiber statistics. || F2-CAMPAIGN L3 RECURSION (2026-07-09, f2_arcclass_recursion PROVED): the structured-arc classes collapse by orthogonality to order-1 censuses (exact regrouping, verified at 6 cells); the mid-band obligation recurses to t = 1 (#{S: sum(S) = 0} flatness/0-pricing) plus the generic-class mass. M1's instance is now the mildest possible. Catch #3 (design-note over-claim at official scale) banked and corrected. || F2-CAMPAIGN CATCHES #4+#5 (2026-07-09 late, M1-t1 profile run): (#4) the Weil-Newton reach table's 'official rows' phrasing QUARANTINED — computed at q = mn+1 small-m shapes; F2's official sub-balance rows have q^t >= 2^n with large t (catch-#11 scale), instantiation open pending the official (q,n,t) table. (#5) the m=1 anchor rows PROVE mid-band signed cancellation is astronomical (exact identity |E_b| = 1 vs float partial products ~2^93): float/absolute Fourier methods are STRUCTURALLY BLIND at mid-band — session-2 machinery must be exact-arithmetic (integer DP / cyclotomic mod-p); valid small-row data shows per-arc mid-band values far below sqrt-flat (R_mid 0.0001-1.7). The t=1 orbit collapse + factorization identity (product of the m orbit polynomials = (1+z^q)/(1+z), coefficients +-1) are PROVED anchors banked from this run. || F2-CAMPAIGN OFFICIAL-SHAPE RESOLUTION (2026-07-09 close): catch #4 resolved — official rows have LARGE t (~7e10 at prize-max, t*log2 q ~ 2.15e12 > n ~ 1.1e12, window empty at every b); f2_newton_empty_extremes (PROVED) empties the bands b <= t and b >= n-t outright; Weil-Newton has zero reach at this shape (stays banked for small-t). The genuinely open content is now: MID-BAND (t < b < n-t) fiber-to-mean ratio <= ~2^{1.05e12} — an exponentially weak anti-concentration target. The floor's remaining heart, exactly posed. || F2-CAMPAIGN CATCH #6 (2026-07-09, self-caught within the hour, corrected in place): the empty-extremes band width at official rows is min(t, q-1) ~ 2^31 per side, NOT t ~ 2^36 — Newton inversion is char-limited (b < q); Frobenius redundancy p_{qj} = p_j^q noted for all mid-band p->e dictionary work. Reframed heart unchanged: mid-band fiber-to-mean <= ~2^{1.05e12}, per-condition loss <= ~2^15 wins. || F2-CAMPAIGN KERNEL WIRING (2026-07-10, f2_zero_prefix_q_equivalence PROVED): below char q the level-j census IS the v13 zero-prefix census (two independent DPs, 20 cells exact) — F2's crux is formally an instance of the shared census kernel at the zero prefix. The campaign's excess-intersection statement and row-sharp Q are one object; upstream Q machinery and F5 P9 apply directly. || F2-CAMPAIGN FULL-LADDER DICTIONARY (2026-07-10, f2_full_ladder_dictionary PROVED, exhaustive incl. t > q): t-null <=> reversed-locator coefficients vanish at q-free indices <= min(t,b) (log-derivative, char-free) — the divisor/Q frame now covers the whole official ladder; q-multiples exactly free; effective condition count exact. || F2-CAMPAIGN UNCONDITIONAL EMPTY BAND (2026-07-10): the extreme bands b <= t and b >= n-t are EMPTY unconditionally (squarefree vs p-th power via the dictionary; extension-field-verified beyond char where the Newton proof was silent) — full width t ~ 2^36/side at prize-max restored with a correct proof; catch #6's width limit superseded. Mid-band = t < b < n-t exactly; per-condition-loss target unchanged. || F2-CAMPAIGN BEZOUT CERTIFICATE (2026-07-10, f2_bezout_certificate PROVED): every t-null block/complement pair is an X-power Bezout pair of divisors of 1 - X^n with unique degree-bounded cofactors — the explicit certificate structure for the face-A rigidity route. || F2-CAMPAIGN R5 s=3 RUNG (2026-07-10, f2_generalized_chord_lemma PROVED): 3-sparse root counts on mu_n obey Z <= gcd(d2,d3,n)*(sqrt(q)+3) — collapse factorization scale-free + exact ambient-annihilator Jacobi formula; catch #7 (wrong annihilator group) caught by verifier pre-banking. || F2-CAMPAIGN CHAR-0 CLASSIFICATION (2026-07-10, f2_char0_sixterm_classification PROVED): the 6-term fiber-coincidence relation has no nondegenerate char-0 solutions (Lam-Leung + matchings, exactly verified); finite-q coincidences are p-divisibility accidents of fixed nonzero cyclotomic integers; official-regime conjecture sharpened to C(3) <= 2 at q >> n^2. The base lemma's char-0 half is closed. || F2-CAMPAIGN TOWER THEOREM (2026-07-10, f2_trinomial_tower_theorem PROVED): Z <= min_j 2^j(2 span_j + 2) for every trinomial on mu_{2^s}, any characteristic — complete elementary proof (coset split + unit substitution + Laurent degree bound, collisions closed), soundness-verified on 1200 trinomials. The mid-band edge's root-count engine; Part 2 (span estimate C sqrt n) and the s-sparse extension scoped. || F2-CAMPAIGN TRINOMIAL THEOREM COMPLETE (2026-07-10): Z <= 4n^{2/3} unconditional (tower + Dirichlet-parity descent, both parts proved and verified) — the s = 3 base of the mid-band edge is now a finished theorem with nine bits of official room. Remaining: s-sparse extension (verbatim proof + application-shape span), edge lemma, crux assembly, L5. || F2-CAMPAIGN s-SPARSE EXTENSION (2026-07-10, f2_ssparse_tower_extension PROVED): Z <= struct-mass + tower bound for every non-collapsed s-sparse polynomial — the theorem splits along the struct/extras line by construction (full-coset vanishing = binomial factors). The edge lemma = this theorem at the edge divisor shapes; then crux assembly, then L5. || F2-CAMPAIGN DETERMINATION LEMMA (2026-07-10, f2_residual_determination_lemma PROVED): extras residuals are uniquely determined by their structured cores (unit-triangular, rho < p) — the edge count = a deterministic-test census over cores (consistency rows + splitting filter). Catch #12 corrected same-cycle. || F2-CAMPAIGN EDGE LEMMA PROVED (2026-07-10, f2_edge_lemma, fourteenth satellite): every edge-size t-null block is a union of full cosets — ZERO extras on the edge band, the dichotomy proved there by the dictionary/shape/determination/semigroup chain. Mid-band interior (rho >= p) = the remaining open; the rho < p boundary is the frontier. The floor's first proved band. || F2-CAMPAIGN k=1 CONTRACTION THEOREM (2026-07-10, f2_k1_contraction_theorem PROVED): per-condition loss L <= 4 at prime-field rows (n >= 512, n >= 3 sqrt p) via our Lemma A + threshold-free Halasz — catch #19 eliminated; the NTT row (12289, 2048) is an empirical stage. The prime-field dichotomy is one loop-induction write-up from end-to-end; the k=2 breakage is the summit's minimal probe. || F2-CAMPAIGN PER-CONDITION 1+o(1) (2026-07-10): the fixed-functional anti-concentration is effectively proved (LMN Esseen chain + our level-set floor: rho <= 1/p + e^{-n/15}); the prime-field flatness reduces to the frequency-space struct ledger — the campaign's collapse machinery in dual coordinates.
nodes.notes: Born red: this hypothesis was always load-bearing, hidden in prose. Compression forced the declaration. | COMPLEMENTATION LEVER (verified 2026-07-06, 27762/27762 at t=2,3,4, mu_16/F_97): S subset of a full mu_n-coset C is t-null IFF C\S is t-null -- because prod_{x in C}(1+ux) = 1 +- delta u^n is SPARSE, so the first t<n coefficients of prod_S equal complete-homogeneous functions of the complement; Newton converts. => the GIANT regime reduces to classifying SMALL t-null sets, where Vandermonde rigidity (proved, support >= t+1) bites and the toy classification is COMPLETE (t=2,|T|<=6: exactly the four mu_4-cosets, nothing else). ATTACK PLAN: (a) prove the sparse-product/Newton complementation lemma (elementary); (b) classify small t-null sets by rigidity+induction (toy: exhaustive match); (c) dichotomy follows for giant blocks. This is the most tractable-looking red on the board. | GOAL OUTCOME 2026-07-06: FALSIFIED as stated (verified giant witness), salvaged by the explicit sub-balance regime hypothesis; complementation lemma + rigidity corollaries PROVED and banked. Difficulty re-ranked: the repaired form is anti-concentration-hard, no longer the easiest red.
nodes.falsifier: Pre-registered: sub-balance (q^t >= 2^n) scaled rows with non-coset-union extras exceeding the transported n^3 budget, sustained across >= 3 scales. Above-balance window accidents do NOT count.

nodes.title: FLOOR CONJECTURE: fully-stripped primitive residue <= n^3 at official corridor rows (already the floor)
nodes.statement: FLOOR RE-POSE (2026-07-07, floor campaign): this node was already floor-shaped (created 2026-07-06 as the weakest x4 premise; consumer sufficiency proved at 16n^3). Now framed as a standing conjecture with the pre-registered falsifier below. DOC CORRECTION BANKED: x4's statement.md prose still prints the stale n^2 primitive target and names u1_pullback_dichotomy — the DAG edge and this n^3 form are authoritative. Opening evidence: budget probe PASS at 1.5% of budget with positive control detected. | At the official X4/list corridor rows, after quotient, dihedral, moment-trade, U2 boundary, and DLI/skew strips have removed their paid columns, the remaining primitive exact-list active-core residue fits inside the direct row column consumed by x4_exactlist_staircase_split. A sufficient concrete target is <= n^3 at the official rows. This is strictly weaker than u1_pullback_dichotomy: it does not classify every canonical trade into a pullback dictionary and does not require the older orbit-converted n^2 survivor bound. | SURVIVAL +1 scoped (2026-07-07, F3-A1): boundary sweep with exact instrument gate (positive control 60 trades) — zero nontoral trades, zero n^3 alarms at 11 rows incl. FULL-window versions of the previously sliced boundary rows and extended primes 3137/12289; n=64 rows clean-but-partial, queued for anchor-sharded F3-A1b. | SURVIVAL +3 -> **HARDENED** (2026-07-07, F3-A2): adversarially smooth q (incl. Fermat 65537) zero trades at full windows; exceptional structure confined to the tiny-field window (zero at p >= 97). Families: boundary sweep / smooth-q engineering / exceptional confinement. Remaining follow-up: n=64 full windows (numpy rewrite). | SURVIVAL +4 (2026-07-07, Pro floor-window 3): independent Pro anchored-PTE probe — gate reproduced exactly, all banked rows re-verified, and our open n=64 flank EXTENDED (complete exact h=5,6 sweeps at the first four primes above n^2: zero non-toral hits). Remaining flank by both sides' accounting: n=64, h >= 7. || FLIP-CHAIN ADDENDUM (2026-07-09, overnight flip branch integrated at merge daa30d2): h=3 obligation provably reduced (f3_h3_officialrow_conditional_close) to two named gates — conic rank deficit (Delta <= 2899 via kernel-bundle splitting, margin 52 of slack in the balanced form) + bridge rank-capacity (Z_4096_floor units) — under the official-row retarget H3-ACT(4096) (n = 2^s kills the toral term). Independent backup lanes: moment program (six star gates + M-bound), private-linear alternate (Delta <= 25). Remaining for the red beyond h=3: h=5 n=64 certificate completion, h=8 residual certification, h-ladder coverage audit.
nodes.attack_surface: anchored same-moment/PTE active-core families over mu_n at q >= n^2, especially boundary p = 1 mod n rows where stronger hn or n^2 trophies can fail; also the X4 row arithmetic after all explicit staircase columns are charged
nodes.falsifier: Pre-registered: a complete or certified row/slice with more than n^3 fully-stripped non-toral primitive active-core trades (post quotient/dihedral/moment/U2/DLI strips), or row arithmetic showing the direct column no longer fits. Pre-strip counts do NOT count.
nodes.notes: AMBER WEAKENING 2026-07-06: x4_exactlist_staircase_split was rewired to consume this narrower red premise instead of the full u1_pullback_dichotomy. The full U1 theorem remains a live target and route material, but the X4 consumer only needs this direct primitive-column budget. AMBER STRESS 2026-07-06: u1_x4_active_core_budget_probe.py replayed exact small active-core gates and checked complete small rows plus capped boundary slices under a 60s cap; no n^3 direct-column excess was found, and the exceptional active-core control was detected.

nodes.title: v13 Q/BC/SP interface (adapter taxonomy)
nodes.statement: ADAPTER (v13 raw, upstream: przchojecki/rs-mca towards-prize.md (2026-07-01 revision) + cap25 v13 raw notes): the safe-side residual decomposes first-match as paid_tangent + paid_quotient + paid_extension + Q + BC + SP + named residuals, where Q = prefix/quotient flatness, BC = base-field-normalized split-pencil census, SP = primitive shift-pair control. PROVED as a taxonomy: the classes are defined first-match with a named-residual catch-all, so the decomposition is exhaustive by construction; the content lives in the class bounds (our existing strata), not the partition. This is the translation bridge: Q ~ our quotient/strip lane, BC ~ our SPI/XR aperiodic census, SP ~ our moment-trade/shift-pair discoveries.

nodes.title: v13 base-field normalization guard (ledger law)
nodes.statement: LEDGER LAW (upstream: przchojecki/rs-mca towards-prize.md (2026-07-01 revision) + cap25 v13 raw notes): prefix-floor and split-pencil entropy costs are normalized over the generated/base field B, NOT over a larger line/challenge field, unless an explicit transfer theorem is wired. PROVED as a convention+guard: any ledger entry spending |F| where the construction only earns |B| is invalid without a cited transfer. Rationale: prevents the false-proof class where extension-field size is spent against base-field-generated objects (the v13 margins are 3.1-22.2 bits -- a |F|/|B| confusion is fatal at that precision). | GUARD CONFIRMED IN THE FIELD (2026-07-07/08, catches #11-13): the M_B seam audit and beta-round found exactly the failures this guard predicts, in F2's window (q^t vs |B0|^t), F7's count law (K_cell/q^sigma vs p^sigma for base-valued worst words, census-verified), and F1's flatness clause (F_q-conditions actually F_p(zeta)-conditions). Statements corrected; evidence untouched. Guard edges added into the exposed reds per the 2026-07-08 alignment packet.

nodes.title: v13 finite adjacent compiler (endpoint convention)
nodes.statement: COMPILER THEOREM (upstream: przchojecki/rs-mca towards-prize.md (2026-07-01 revision) + cap25 v13 raw notes): if the exact unsafe inequality holds at agreement a0, and the safe upper ledger at a1 = a0+1 is a disjoint/first-match integer sum bounded by B*, then a1 is the first safe agreement and the closed-radius threshold is pinned at the a0/a1 boundary. PROVED (one paragraph): unsafe(a0) gives count > B* at a0; the ledger gives count <= B* at a1; counts are monotone in agreement; hence the crossing is exactly between a0 and a1 -- the adjacent determination in the sense of the prize spec and of our mca_grand statement. Matches the F_17^32 pinned row (LD 7 at 506, 6 at 507).

nodes.title: v13 prefix collision ledger (upstream, audit pending)
nodes.statement: UPSTREAM CLAIM held at TARGET pending independent replay (quarantine precedent, standing order 12; upstream: przchojecki/rs-mca towards-prize.md (2026-07-01 revision) + cap25 v13 raw notes): equal prefix fibers imply strong split rigidity; prefix collisions decompose into quotient-pullback and primitive shift-pair pieces; the exact second moment is a collision ledger over split locators. If audited green, this feeds our q_recursion/strip/gap1 lane as a second route to the prefix-fiber second moment. Does NOT by itself give max-fiber control (so it cannot close gap1_noneq_mass).

nodes.title: v13 BC = split-pencil normal form (identification)
nodes.statement: IDENTIFICATION CONJECTURE (upstream: przchojecki/rs-mca towards-prize.md (2026-07-01 revision) + cap25 v13 raw notes): after removing tangent, quotient, extension, common-GCD, and bounded chart strata, the remaining aperiodic safe-side problem IS a base-field split-pencil incidence/census problem -- i.e. upstream BC and our SPI/XR/M5 machinery are the same object. Untested as an exact identification (quantifier/row alignment unchecked -- the tr_perleaf lesson applies); held CONJECTURE until a transport check like the one run for tr_perleaf_list_ident passes.

nodes.title: v13 SP: shift-pair residual is a distinct class
nodes.statement: HYGIENE CONJECTURE (upstream: przchojecki/rs-mca towards-prize.md (2026-07-01 revision) + cap25 v13 raw notes): primitive shift-pair coincidences form a residual class DISTINCT from quotient-pullback pairs and must not be hidden inside Q or BC. Aligns with our verified moment-trade / fifth-mechanism discoveries (the x4 moment column; Pro round-5 engineered relation orbits are exactly primitive shift-pair objects). Prevents the DAG from pretending all cancellation is quotient symmetry.

nodes.title: v13 frontier row packets (deployed edges + adjacent predictions)
nodes.statement: BENCHMARK/REGRESSION (upstream: przchojecki/rs-mca towards-prize.md (2026-07-01 revision) + cap25 v13 raw notes, all RAW/PRE-AUDIT per upstream caveat): (i) deployed v13 unsafe edges: KoalaBear sextic MCA delta >= 0.4678273, Mersenne-31 circle MCA delta >= 0.4678388 (improving the v12 public edge 0.467865); (ii) the four adjacent predictions: KoalaBear unsafe at 1116047 / conjectured first safe 1116048; Mersenne-31 unsafe 1116023 / conjectured 1116024; next-step margins 22.2, 22.0, 3.3, 3.1 bits (an unspecified poly(n) loss is NOT a finite certificate at these margins); (iii) frontier-adjacent packet JSONs + identity-prefix unsafe certificates as regression tests. NOT critical proof mass; useful as harness/row-slate test material.
nodes.notes: TEST-STATUS RETIRED 2026-07-06 (node-semantics law: every node is a truth claim). This node is now the CLAIM about its computation; a computation claim is PROVED only with a pinned result + replayable verifier in the node folder (none exists yet -- run evidence, where any, is anecdotal notes). Obligation: pin + replay, then flip to PROVED-by-computation.

nodes.title: Upstream headline conjecture: entropy-subfield envelope
nodes.statement: UPSTREAM HEADLINE CONJECTURE (upstream: przchojecki/rs-mca towards-prize.md (2026-07-01 revision) + cap25 v13 raw notes): the closed-form threshold delta*_C(eps*) = 1 - rho - g*(rho,beta) + o(1) (the entropy-subfield envelope). This is the upstream program-level answer-shape our conjectural_mca_delta.py row map should converge to; kept as a named CONJECTURE for visibility in the shared artifact. Consistent with our corridor arithmetic (batch-1 crossings) at the four rates.

nodes.title: C1' DYADIC-K (ledger form): near-peak mass <= K' * r * (1 + W_cl(row)), K' <= 4
